item.title: 🚀 推出 ✨ Bose Framework - 机器人开发人员的瑞士军刀 🤖 | 🚀 Introducing ✨ Bose Framework - The Swiss Army Knife for Bot Developers 🤖
item.description:
机器人开发很艰难。

像 Cloudflare 这样的机器人检测器已准备好保护网站免受我们的机器人攻击。 使用 ChromeOptions 配置 Selenium 以指定驱动程序路径、配置文件、用户代理和窗口大小是繁琐的，并且在 Windows 中是一场噩梦。 通过日志调试 Bot Crashes 很困难。 您如何在不牺牲速度和便捷开发的情况下解决这些痛点？

输入 Bose。 它由 Selenium 提供支持，提供了一系列特性和功能来简化机器人开发过程。 据我们所知，Bose 是该镇首个同类机器人开发框架。

入门

克隆入门模板

git clone https://github.com/omkarcloud/bose-starter my-bose-project

然后切换到该目录，安装依赖项，并启动项目：

 cd my-bose-project python -m pip install -r requirements.txt python main.py

第一次运行需要一些时间，因为它会下载 chrome 驱动程序可执行文件，后续运行会很快。

核心功能

  1. 添加强大的方法，使使用 Selenium 变得更加容易。
  2. 遵循最佳实践以避免被 Cloudflare 和 PerimeterX 检测到机器人程序。
  3. 保存每个任务运行的 HTML、屏幕截图和运行详细信息，以便于调试。
  4. 将抓取的数据写入 JSON、CSV 和 Excel 文件的实用程序组件。
  5. 自动下载并初始化正确的 Chrome 驱动程序。
  6. 快速且对开发人员友好。

用法

假设您想开始抓取网站。 如果您使用裸 Selenium，则必须像这样处理打开和关闭驱动程序的必要任务：

 from selenium import webdriver driver_path = 'path/to/chromedriver' driver = webdriver.Chrome(executable_path=driver_path) driver.get('https://www.example.com') driver.quit()

但是，借助 Bose Framework，您可以采用声明式和结构化的方法。 您只需编写以下代码，Bose 驱动程序将负责创建驱动程序，将其传递给 Task 的run方法，并关闭驱动程序：

 from bose import * class Task(BaseTask): def run(self, driver): driver.get('https://www.example.com')

配置

在裸 Selenium 中，如果要配置配置文件、用户代理或窗口大小等选项，则需要编写大量代码，如下所示：

 from selenium.webdriver.chrome.options import Options from selenium import webdriver driver_path = 'path/to/chromedriver.exe' options = Options() profile_path = '1' options.add_argument(f'--user-data-dir={profile_path}') user_agent = 'Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/106.0.0.0 Safari/537.37")' options.add_argument(f'--user-agent={user_agent}') window_width = 1200 window_height = 720 options.add_argument(f'--window-size={window_width},{window_height}') driver = webdriver.Chrome(executable_path=driver_path, options=options)

另一方面，Bose 框架通过将浏览器配置封装在任务的BrowserConfig属性中来简化这些复杂性，如下所示：

 from bose import BaseTask, BrowserConfig, UserAgent, WindowSize class Task(BaseTask): browser_config = BrowserConfig(user_agent=UserAgent.user_agent_106, window_size=WindowSize.window_size_1280_720, profile=1)

异常处理

使用 Selenium 时异常很常见。 在裸 Selenium 中，如果发生异常，驱动程序会自动关闭，只留下可供调试的日志。

在 Bose 中，当抓取任务发生异常时，浏览器保持打开状态，而不是立即关闭。 这使您可以看到异常发生时的实时浏览器状态，这对调试有很大帮助。

调试

使用原始 Selenium 进行调试时，您可能必须筛选日志才能确定问题。 幸运的是，Bose 通过存储有关每次运行的信息让您可以轻松进行调试。

每次运行后，都会在任务中创建一个目录，其中包含三个文件，如下所列：

task_info.json

它包含有关任务运行的信息，例如任务运行的持续时间、任务的 ip 详细信息、用户代理、window_size 和用于执行任务的配置文件。

final.png

这是在关闭驱动程序之前捕获的屏幕截图。

page.html

这是在关闭驱动程序之前捕获的 html 源代码。 如果您的选择器无法选择元素，知道这一点非常有用。

error.log

如果您的任务因异常而崩溃，我们还会存储 error.log，其中包含导致任务崩溃的错误。 这对调试很有帮助。

输出数据

执行网页抓取后，我们需要以 JSON 或 CSV 格式存储数据。 通常，此过程涉及编写大量命令式代码，如下所示：

 import csv import json def write_json(data, filename): with open(filename, 'w') as fp: json.dump(data, fp, indent=4) def write_csv(data, filename): with open(filename, 'w', newline='', encoding='utf-8') as csvfile: fieldnames = data[0].keys() # get the fieldnames from the first dictionary writer = csv.DictWriter(csvfile, fieldnames=fieldnames) writer.writeheader() # write the header row writer.writerows(data) # write each row of data data = [ { "text": "\u201cThe world as we have created it is a process of our thinking. It cannot be changed without changing our thinking.\u201d", "author": "Albert Einstein" }, { "text": "\u201cIt is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.\u201d", "author": "JK Rowling" } ] write_json(data, "data.json") write_csv(data, "data.csv")

要使用输出方法，请为要保存的文件类型调用write方法。

所有数据将保存在output/文件夹中：

请参阅以下代码以供参考

from bose import Output data = [ { "text": "\u201cThe world as we have created it is a process of our thinking. It cannot be changed without changing our thinking.\u201d", "author": "Albert Einstein" }, { "text": "\u201cIt is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.\u201d", "author": "JK Rowling" } ] Output.write_json(data, "data.json") Output.write_csv(data, "data.csv")

未检测到的驱动程序

在 Bose Framework 中使用 Undetected Driver 就像将use_undetected_driver选项传递给BrowserConfig一样简单，如下所示：

 from bose import BaseTask, BrowserConfig class Task(BaseTask): browser_config = BrowserConfig(use_undetected_driver=True)

本地存储

就像现代浏览器如何拥有本地存储模块一样，Bose 也在其框架中融入了相同的概念。

您可以从 Bose 导入 LocalStorage 对象以跨浏览器运行持久保存数据，这在抓取大量数据时非常有用。

数据存储在项目根目录中名为local_storage.json的文件中。 以下是您可以如何使用它：

 from bose import LocalStorage LocalStorage.set_item("pages", 5) print(LocalStorage.get_item("pages"))

老板司机

您在 Task 的run方法中收到的驱动程序是 Selenium 的扩展版本，它添加了强大的方法，使使用 Selenium 变得更加容易。 方法描述模拟一次访问，就像您通过单击链接到达该页面一样。 这种方法创建了一种更自然且不易察觉的浏览行为。 使您能够使用 JavaScript 单击元素，绕过弹出窗口或警报的任何拦截（ElementClickInterceptedException） 返回包含“cookies”和“local_storage”的字典将 cookie 和本地存储数据添加到当前网站访问谷歌然后访问“链接”使其不易被发现本地存储返回 LocalStorage 模块的实例，以便以易于使用的方式与浏览器的本地存储进行交互将当前网页的屏幕截图保存到 tasks/ 目录中的文件中睡眠随机时间，2 到 4 秒（短）或 6 到 9 秒（长） 根据不同的标准在页面上查找网页元素。 如果 web 元素存在，它们将返回，如果不存在，则返回 None。 检查浏览器是否在指定页面

祝您使用 Bose Framework 愉快地进行机器人开发！

了解更多

要详细了解 Bose Bot 开发框架，请阅读www.omkar.cloud/bose/上的 Bose 文档

如果 Bose Framework 对 Bot 开发有帮助，请花点时间为存储库加注星标。 您的星标行为将帮助开发人员发现我们的存储库，并为帮助 Bot 开发中的其他开发人员做出贡献。 丹亚瓦德🙏！ 范德马塔兰！



原文:

Bot Development is Tough.

Bot Detectors like Cloudflare are ready to defend websites from our Bots. Configuring Selenium with ChromeOptions to specify the driver path, profile, user agent, and window size is Cumbersome and a nightmare in windows. Debugging Bot Crashes via logs is hard. How do you solve these pain points without sacrificing speed and handy development?

Enter Bose. Bose is the first bot development framework in the Developer Community that is specifically designed to provide the best developer experience for bot developers. Powered by Selenium, it offers a range of features and functionalities to simplify the process of bot development. As far as our knowledge goes, Bose is the first bot development framework of its kind in the town.

Getting Started

Clone Starter Template

git clone https://github.com/omkarcloud/bose-starter my-bose-project

Then change into that directory, install dependencies, and start the project:

cd my-bose-project
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt
python main.py

The first run will take some time as it downloads the chrome driver executable, subsequent runs will be fast.

Core features

  1. Adds Powerful Methods to make working with Selenium a lot easier.
  2. Follows best practices to avoid Bot Detection by Cloudflare and PerimeterX.
  3. Saves the HTML, Screenshot, and the run details for each task run to enable easy debugging.
  4. Utility components to write scraped data as JSON, CSV, and Excel files.
  5. Automatically downloads and initializes the correct Chrome driver.
  6. Fast and Developer friendly.

Usage

Say you want to start scraping a website. If you were using bare Selenium, you would have to handle the imperative tasks of opening and closing the driver like this:

from selenium import webdriver driver_path = 'path/to/chromedriver' driver = webdriver.Chrome(executable_path=driver_path) driver.get('https://www.example.com') driver.quit()

However, with Bose Framework, you can take a declarative and structured approach. You only need to write the following code, and Bose driver will take care of creating the driver, passing it to the run method of the Task, and closing the driver:

from bose import * class Task(BaseTask): def run(self, driver): driver.get('https://www.example.com')

Configuration

In bare Selenium, if you want to configure options such as the profile, user agent, or window size, it requires writing a lot of code, as shown below:

from selenium.webdriver.chrome.options import Options
from selenium import webdriver driver_path = 'path/to/chromedriver.exe' options = Options() profile_path = '1' options.add_argument(f'--user-data-dir={profile_path}') user_agent = 'Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/106.0.0.0 Safari/537.37")'
options.add_argument(f'--user-agent={user_agent}') window_width = 1200
window_height = 720
options.add_argument(f'--window-size={window_width},{window_height}') driver = webdriver.Chrome(executable_path=driver_path, options=options)

On the other hand, Bose Framework simplifies these complexities by encapsulating the browser configuration within the BrowserConfig property of the Task, as shown below:

from bose import BaseTask, BrowserConfig, UserAgent, WindowSize class Task(BaseTask): browser_config = BrowserConfig(user_agent=UserAgent.user_agent_106, window_size=WindowSize.window_size_1280_720, profile=1)

Exception handling

Exceptions are common when using Selenium. In bare Selenium, if an exception occurs, the driver automatically closes, leaving you with only logs to debug.

In Bose, when an exception occurs in a scraping task, the browser remains open instead of immediately closing. This allows you to see the live browser state at the moment the exception occurred, which greatly helps in debugging.

Debugging

Web scraping can often be fraught with errors, such as incorrect selectors or pages that fail to load. When debugging with raw Selenium, you may have to sift through logs to identify the issue. Fortunately, Bose makes it simple for you to debug by storing information about each run.

After each run a directory is created in tasks which contains three files, which are listed below:

task_info.json

It contains information about the task run such as duration for which the task run, the ip details of task, the user agent, window_size and profile which used to execute the task.

final.png

This is the screenshot captured before driver was closed.

page.html

This is the html source captured before driver was closed. Very useful to know in case your selectors failed to select elements.

error.log

In case your task crashed due to exception we also store error.log which contains the error due to which the task crashed. This is very helful in debugging.

Outputting Data

After performing web scraping, we need to store the data in either JSON or CSV format. Typically, this process involves writing a significant amount of imperative code which looks like this:

import csv
import json def write_json(data, filename): with open(filename, 'w') as fp: json.dump(data, fp, indent=4) def write_csv(data, filename): with open(filename, 'w', newline='', encoding='utf-8') as csvfile: fieldnames = data[0].keys() # get the fieldnames from the first dictionary
 writer = csv.DictWriter(csvfile, fieldnames=fieldnames) writer.writeheader() # write the header row
 writer.writerows(data) # write each row of data

data = [ { "text": "\u201cThe world as we have created it is a process of our thinking. It cannot be changed without changing our thinking.\u201d", "author": "Albert Einstein" }, { "text": "\u201cIt is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.\u201d", "author": "J.K. Rowling" }
] write_json(data, "data.json")
write_csv(data, "data.csv")

Bose simplifies these complexities by encapsulating them in Output Module for reading and writing Data.

To use Output Method, call the write method for the type of file you want to save.

All data will be saved in the output/ folder:

See following Code for Reference

from bose import Output data = [ { "text": "\u201cThe world as we have created it is a process of our thinking. It cannot be changed without changing our thinking.\u201d", "author": "Albert Einstein" }, { "text": "\u201cIt is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.\u201d", "author": "J.K. Rowling" }
] Output.write_json(data, "data.json")
Output.write_csv(data, "data.csv")

Undetected Driver

Ultrafunkamsterdam created a ChromeDriver that has excellent support for bypassing all major bot detection systems such as Distil, Datadome, Cloudflare, and others.

Bose recognized the importance of bypassing bot detections and provides in built support for Ultrafunkamsterdam’s Undetected Driver

Using the Undetected Driver in Bose Framework is as simple as passing the use_undetected_driver option to the BrowserConfig, like so:

from bose import BaseTask, BrowserConfig class Task(BaseTask): browser_config = BrowserConfig(use_undetected_driver=True)

LocalStorage

Just like how modern browsers have a local storage module, Bose has also incorporated the same concept in its framework.

You can import the LocalStorage object from Bose to persist data across browser runs, which is extremely useful when scraping large amounts of data.

The data is stored in a file named local_storage.json in the root directory of your project. Here's how you can use it:

from bose import LocalStorage LocalStorage.set_item("pages", 5)
print(LocalStorage.get_item("pages"))

Boss Driver

The driver you receive in the run method of the Task is an extended version of Selenium that adds powerful methods to make working with Selenium much easier. Some of the popular methods added to the Selenium driver by Bose Framework are:

METHOD DESCRIPTION
get_by_current_page_referrer(link, wait=None) simulate a visit that appears as if you arrived at the page by clicking a link. This approach creates a more natural and less detectable browsing behavior.
js_click(element) enables you to click on an element using JavaScript, bypassing any interceptions(ElementClickInterceptedException) from pop-ups or alerts
get_cookies_and_local_storage_dict() returns a dictionary containing "cookies" and "local_storage”
add_cookies_and_local_storage_dict(self, site_data) adds both cookies and local storage data to the current web site
organic_get(link, wait=None) visits google and then visits the “link” making it less detectable
local_storage returns an instance of the LocalStorage module for interacting with the browser's local storage in an easy to use manner
save_screenshot(filename=None) save a screenshot of the current web page to a file in tasks/ directory
short_random_sleep() and long_random_sleep(): sleep for a random amount of time, either between 2 and 4 seconds (short) or between 6 and 9 seconds (long)
get_element_or_* [eg: get_element_or_none, get_element_or_none_by_selector, get_element_by_id, get_element_or_none_by_text_contains,] find web elements on the page based on different criteria. They return the web element if it exists, or None if it doesn't.
is_in_page(target, wait=None, raise_exception=False) checks if the browser is in the specified page

Bose is an excellent framework that simplifies the boring parts of Selenium and web scraping.

Wish you best of Luck and Happy Bot Development with Bose Framework!

Learn More

To learn about Bose Bot Development Framework in detail, read the Bose docs at www.omkar.cloud/bose/

If Bose Framework helped in Bot Development, please take a moment to star the repository. Your act of starring will help developers in discovering our Repository and contribute towards helping fellow developers in Bot Development. Dhanyawad 🙏! Vande Mataram!
item.author: chetanam

item.title: 🌟🌟 Show Your Support: Please Star my GitHub Repositories! 🌟🌟 表达您的支持：请为我的 GitHub 存储库加注星标！ 🌟🌟 🌟🌟 | 🌟🌟 Show Your Support: Please Star my GitHub Repositories! 🌟🌟
item.description:
合十礼🙏,

你们中的一些人可能知道，我一直在 GitHub 上不知疲倦地从事几个令人兴奋的项目，如果您能花点时间为存储库加注星标，我将不胜感激。

我已将我的心血倾注到两个存储库中，它们是：

您的主演行为将帮助开发人员发现这些存储库，并有助于帮助其他开发人员满足他们的机器人开发需求。

丹亚瓦德🙏

一旦暂停，chetanam 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，chetanam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，chetanam 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 chetanam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 chetanam：

使 chetanam 的所有帖子不那么显眼

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 chetanam 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

   

Namaste 🙏,

As some of you may know, I've been working tirelessly on a couple of exciting projects on GitHub, and I would be immensely grateful if you could take a moment to star the repositories.

I've poured my heart and soul into two repositories, and they are:

Your act of starring will help developers in discovering these Repository and contribute towards helping fellow developers in their Bot Development Needs.

Dhanyawad 🙏

Viktoria Bors-Pajuste - May 9

Pachi 🪐 (she/her/ela) - May 18

Once suspended, chetanam will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, chetanam will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by chetanam will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If chetanam is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag chetanam:

Make all posts by chetanam less visible

chetanam consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging chetanam will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: chetanam

item.title: 管理数据驻留 - 演示 | Managing Data Residency - the demo
item.description:
我在上一篇文章中解释了数据驻留背后的概念和理论。 是时候亲自动手并在一个简单的演示中实现它了。

示例架构

在上一篇文章的最后一节中，我提出了一个示例架构，其中基于位置的路由发生在两个不同的阶段：

  1. 根据其值，它将请求转发到计算的上游； 如果没有找到值或没有匹配的值，它会将它转发到默认的上游。
  2. 该应用程序使用Apache Shardingsphre根据数据再次进行路由。 如果 API 网关计算的值是正确的，则流保持“在其通道内”； 如果不是，它会被路由到正确的数据库，但由于它在它的通道之外，所以性能会下降。

我简化了一些方面：

  • 该理论使用两个 API 网关实例。 对于演示，我只使用了一个。
  • 请记住，该位置不是在第一次请求时在客户端设置的。 它应该与第一个响应一起返回、存储并由客户端在后续调用中重复使用。 我没有费心实施客户端。
  • 我喜欢我的演示是独立的，所以我没有使用任何云提供商。

这是最终的组件图：

数据模型很简单：

我们在每个数据库中插入特定于位置的数据：

 INSERT INTO europe.owner VALUES ('dujardin', 'fr', 'Jean Dujardin'); INSERT INTO europe.thingy VALUES (1, 'Croissant', 'dujardin'); INSERT INTO usa.owner VALUES ('wayne', 'us', 'John Wayne'); INSERT INTO usa.thingy VALUES (2, 'Lasso', 'wayne');

最后，我们开发了一个简单的 RESTful API 来获取东西：

  • GET /thingies/
  • GET /thingies/{id}

现在我们已经搭建好了舞台，让我们检查如何在两个级别上实现路由。

您也可以将两者结合起来。 我选择前者，因为它最容易设置。 有关它们之间的比较，请查看此表。

第一步是将依赖项添加到 POM：

 <dependency> <groupId>org.apache.shardingsphere</groupId> <artifactId>shardingsphere-jdbc-core</artifactId> <version>5.3.2</version> </dependency>

我们必须配置框架才能使用它。 对于 Spring Boot，它看起来像下面这样：

 spring: datasource: driver-class-name: org.apache.shardingsphere.driver.ShardingSphereDriver #1 url: jdbc:shardingsphere:absolutepath:/etc/sharding.yml #2-3
  1. 兼容 JDBC 的 ShardingSphere 驱动程序
  2. 配置文件
  3. 与文档所说的相反，完整的前缀是jdbc:shardingsphere:absolutepath 。 我已经打开了一个PR来修复文档。

下一步是使用数据源配置 ShardingSphere 本身：

 dataSources: #1 europe: dataSourceClassName: com.zaxxer.hikari.HikariDataSource driverClassName: org.postgresql.Driver jdbcUrl: "jdbc:postgresql://dbeurope:5432/postgres?currentSchema=europe" username: postgres password: root usa: dataSourceClassName: com.zaxxer.hikari.HikariDataSource driverClassName: org.postgresql.Driver jdbcUrl: "jdbc:postgresql://dbusa:5432/postgres?currentSchema=usa" username: postgres password: root rules: #2 - !SHARDING tables: owner: #3 actualDataNodes: europe.owner,usa.owner #4 tableStrategy: standard: shardingColumn: country #3 shardingAlgorithmName: by_country #5 shardingAlgorithms: by_country: type: CLASS_BASED #6 props: strategy: STANDARD algorithmClassName: ch.frankel.blog.dataresidency.LocationBasedSharding #7
  1. 定义两个数据源， europe和usa
  2. 定义规则。 许多规则可用； 我们只会使用分片在欧洲和美国位置之间拆分数据
  3. 分片发生在owner表的country列上
  4. 实际碎片
  5. 要使用的算法。 当我们想要一个特定的拆分时，我们定义了我们自己的
  6. 设置算法类型
  7. 引用自定义算法类

最后一步是提供算法的代码：

 class LocationBasedSharding : StandardShardingAlgorithm<String> { //1 override fun doSharding(targetNames: MutableCollection<String>, shardingValue: PreciseShardingValue<String>) = when (shardingValue.value) { //2 "fr" -> "europe" "us" -> "usa" else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("No sharding over ${shardingValue.value} defined") } }
  1. 继承自StandardShardingAlgorithm<T> ，其中T是分片列的数据类型。 这里是country
  2. 根据分片列的值，返回要使用的数据源名称

综上所述，应用程序将根据所有者所在的国家/地区在相关数据源中获取事物。

我们应该尽早路由以避免欧洲的应用程序实例获取美国数据。 在我们的例子中，它转化为 API 网关阶段的路由。

我将使用 APISIX独立模式进行配置。 让我们定义两个上游：

 upstreams: - id: 1 nodes: "appeurope:8080": 1 - id: 2 nodes: "appusa:8080": 1

现在，我们将定义魔法发生的路线：

 routes: - uri: /thingies* #1 name: Europe upstream_id: 1 vars: [["http_x-country", "==", "fr"]] #2 priority: 3 #3 - uri: /thingies* #4 name: USA upstream_id: 2 vars: [["http_x-country", "==", "us"]] priority: 2 #3 - uri: /thingies* #5 name: default upstream_id: 1 priority: 1 #3
  1. 定义到位于欧洲的应用程序的路线
  2. 这里，条件是X-Country标头具有fr值
  3. 如果请求不匹配，例如，因为标头没有设置值，它会评估优先级列表中的下一条路由。
  4. 定义到位于美国的应用程序的路线
  5. 定义默认路由

第一个请求不带头； 后续请求设置X-Country标头，因为对第一个请求的响应携带了该信息，并且客户端已将其存储。 请记住，它超出了演示的范围。 在大多数情况下，它被设置到正确的位置； 因此，该请求将保持“在其车道上”。 否则，配置的路由仍会在适当的位置找到数据，但代价是在其他通道中获取数据的延迟增加。

在实践中观察流程

检查设计是否按预期运行总是一个好主意。 我们可以为此使用 OpenTelemetry。 有关如何在此类架构中设置 OpenTelemetry 的更多信息，请参阅使用 OpenTelemetry 进行端到端跟踪。

请注意，Apache ShardingSphere 支持 OpenTelemetry 但不提供二进制代理。 您需要从源代码构建它。 我承认我懒得去做。

让我们从无头请求开始：

 curl localhost:9080/thingies/1

它使用 APISIX 中定义的默认路由并返回正确的数据，这要归功于 ShardingSphere。 现在，让我们将国家/地区设置为fr ，这是正确的。

 curl -H 'X-Country: fr' localhost:9080/thingies/1

最后，想象一个恶意行为者更改标头以获取位于美国的数据。

 curl -H 'X-Country: us' localhost:9080/thingies/1

但是，Shardingsphere 仍然从欧洲获取数据。

结论

在上一篇文章中，我解释了数据驻留背后的概念。 在这篇文章中，我在一个简单的架构中实现了它，这要归功于 Apache APISIX 和 Apache ShardingSphere。 该演示简化了现实，但应该是构建生产级数据驻留架构的良好基础。

这篇文章的完整源代码可以在 GitHub 上找到：

更进一步：

最初于 2023 年 5 月 21日发表于A Java Geek



原文:

I explained the concepts and theory behind Data Residency in a previous post. It's time to get our hands dirty and implement it in a simple demo.

The sample architecture

In the last section of the previous post, I proposed a sample architecture where location-based routing happened at two different stages:

  1. The API Gateway checks for an existing X-Country header. Depending on its value, it forwards the request to the computed upstream; If no value is found or no value matches, it forwards it to a default upstream.
  2. The application uses Apache Shardingsphre to route again depending on the data. If the value computed by the API Gateway is correct, the flow stays "in its lane"; if not, it's routed to the correct database, but with a performance penalty as it's outside its lane.

I simplified some aspects:

  • The theory uses two API Gateway instances. For the demo, I used only one.
  • Remember that the location isn't set client-side on the first request. It should be returned along the first response, stored, and reused by the client on subsequent calls. I didn't bother with implementing the client.
  • I like my demos to be self-contained, so I didn't use any Cloud Provider.

Here's the final component diagram:

The data model is simple:

We insert location-specific data on each database:

INSERT INTO europe.owner VALUES ('dujardin', 'fr', 'Jean Dujardin');
INSERT INTO europe.thingy VALUES (1, 'Croissant', 'dujardin'); INSERT INTO usa.owner VALUES ('wayne', 'us', 'John Wayne');
INSERT INTO usa.thingy VALUES (2, 'Lasso', 'wayne');

Finally, we develop a straightforward RESTful API to fetch thingies:

  • GET /thingies/
  • GET /thingies/{id}

Now that we have set the stage, let's check how to implement routing at the two levels.

Routing on Apache ShardingSphere

Apache ShardingSphere offers two approaches: as a library inside the application, ShardingSphere-JDBC, or as a full-fledged deployable component, ShardingSphere-Proxy. You can also combine both. I chose the former because it's the easiest to set up. For a comparison between them, please check this table.

The first step is to add the dependency to the POM:

<dependency> <groupId>org.apache.shardingsphere</groupId> <artifactId>shardingsphere-jdbc-core</artifactId> <version>5.3.2</version>
</dependency>

ShardingSphere-JDBC acts as an indirection layer between the application and the data sources. We must configure the framework to use it. For Spring Boot, it looks like the following:

spring: datasource: driver-class-name: org.apache.shardingsphere.driver.ShardingSphereDriver #1 url: jdbc:shardingsphere:absolutepath:/etc/sharding.yml #2-3
  1. JDBC-compatible ShardingSphere driver
  2. Configuration file
  3. Opposite to what the documentation tells, the full prefix is jdbc:shardingsphere:absolutepath. I've opened a PR to fix the documentation.

The next step is to configure ShardingSphere itself with the data sources:

dataSources: #1 europe: dataSourceClassName: com.zaxxer.hikari.HikariDataSource driverClassName: org.postgresql.Driver jdbcUrl: "jdbc:postgresql://dbeurope:5432/postgres?currentSchema=europe" username: postgres password: root usa: dataSourceClassName: com.zaxxer.hikari.HikariDataSource driverClassName: org.postgresql.Driver jdbcUrl: "jdbc:postgresql://dbusa:5432/postgres?currentSchema=usa" username: postgres password: root
rules: #2 - !SHARDING tables: owner: #3 actualDataNodes: europe.owner,usa.owner #4 tableStrategy: standard: shardingColumn: country #3 shardingAlgorithmName: by_country #5 shardingAlgorithms: by_country: type: CLASS_BASED #6 props: strategy: STANDARD algorithmClassName: ch.frankel.blog.dataresidency.LocationBasedSharding #7
  1. Define the two data sources, europe and usa
  2. Define rules. Many rules are available; we will only use sharding to split data between Europe and USA locations
  3. Sharding happens on the country column of the owner table
  4. Actual shards
  5. Algorithm to use. ShardingSphere offers a couple of algorithms out-of-the-box, which generally try to balance data equally between the sources. As we want a particular split, we define our own
  6. Set the algorithm type
  7. Reference the custom algorithm class

The final step is to provide the algorithm's code:

class LocationBasedSharding : StandardShardingAlgorithm<String> { //1 override fun doSharding(targetNames: MutableCollection<String>, shardingValue: PreciseShardingValue<String>) = when (shardingValue.value) { //2 "fr" -> "europe" "us" -> "usa" else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("No sharding over ${shardingValue.value} defined") }
}
  1. Inherit from StandardShardingAlgorithm<T>, where T is the data type of the sharding column. Here, it's country
  2. Based on the sharding column's value, return the name of the data source to use

With all of the above, the application will fetch thingies in the relevant data source based on the owner's country.

Routing on Apache APISIX

We should route as early as possible to avoid an application instance in Europe fetching US data. In our case, it translates to routing at the API Gateway stage.

I'll use APISIX standalone mode for configuration. Let's define the two upstreams:

upstreams: - id: 1 nodes: "appeurope:8080": 1 - id: 2 nodes: "appusa:8080": 1

Now, we shall define the routes where the magic happens:

routes: - uri: /thingies* #1 name: Europe upstream_id: 1 vars: [["http_x-country", "==", "fr"]] #2 priority: 3 #3 - uri: /thingies* #4 name: USA upstream_id: 2 vars: [["http_x-country", "==", "us"]] priority: 2 #3 - uri: /thingies* #5 name: default upstream_id: 1 priority: 1 #3
  1. Define the route to the Europe-located app
  2. APISIX matches the HTTP methods, the URI and conditions. Here, the condition is that the X-Country header has the fr value
  3. APISIX evaluates matching in priority order, starting with the highest priority. If the request doesn't match, e.g., because the header doesn't have the set value, it evaluates the next route in the priority list.
  4. Define the route to the USA-located app
  5. Define a default route

The first request carries no header; APISIX forwards it to the default route, where ShardingSphere finds data in the relevant data source.

Subsequent requests set the X-Country header because the response to the first request carries the information, and the client has stored it. Remember that it's outside the scope of the demo. In most cases, it's set to the correct location; hence, the request will stay "in its lane". If not, the configured routing will still find the data in the appropriate location at the cost of increased latency to fetch data in the other lane.

Observing the flow in practice

It's always a good idea to check that the design behaves as expected. We can use OpenTelemetry for this. For more information on how to set up OpenTelemetry in such an architecture, please refer to End-to-end tracing with OpenTelemetry.

Note that Apache ShardingSphere supports OpenTelemetry but doesn't provide the binary agent. You need to build it from source. I admit I was too lazy to do it.

Let's start with a headerless request:

curl localhost:9080/thingies/1

It uses the default route defined in APISIX and returns the correct data, thanks to ShardingSphere. Now, let's set the country to fr, which is correct.

curl -H 'X-Country: fr' localhost:9080/thingies/1

APISIX correctly forwards the request to the Europe-located app.

Finally, imagine a malicious actor changing the header to get their hands on data that are located in the US.

curl -H 'X-Country: us' localhost:9080/thingies/1

APISIX forwards it to the USA-located app according to the header. However, Shardingsphere still fetches data from Europe.

Conclusion

In the previous post, I explained the concepts behind Data Residency. In this post, I implemented it within a simple architecture, thanks to Apache APISIX and Apache ShardingSphere. The demo simplifies reality but should be an excellent foundation for building your production-grade Data Residency architecture.

The complete source code for this post can be found on GitHub:

To go further:

Originally published at A Java Geek on May 21th, 2023
item.author: nfrankel

item.title: 本月精选模组：Christine Belzie | Featured Mod of the Month: Christine Belzie
item.description:
在本系列中，我们重点介绍了不同的 DEV 版主——受信任的成员和 Tag Mods——他们帮助使 DEV 成为一个友善、乐于助人的地方。 除了传播良好的氛围和帮助其他社区成员外，这些人还通过根据需要添加和删除标签来帮助我们删除垃圾邮件并保持帖子井井有条。

如果您想了解更多关于这些了不起的人所做的事情，我建议您查看我们的可信会员和标签审核指南。 如果您有兴趣加入成为版主，那么在这两个指南中都有关于如何申请的信息。

介绍 Christine Belzie 🙌

这个月，我们邀请了Christine Belzie ，她作为可信成员和#beginners 、 #opensource和#writing的标签版主协助我们。 她在开源世界倡导包容性和多样性的目标与我们在 DEV 的价值观非常一致，并使她成为我们 mod 团队的优秀成员。 最近有幸对克里斯汀进行了异步采访，很高兴在这里与大家分享。 谢谢你，克里斯汀！

面试

让我们直接进入我的问题和克里斯汀的回答！

一切都进行得很顺利，直到有人问我是否具备 HTML 的基本知识。 我深吸一口气，对面试官说“不”。 几天后，我收到了一封经典的“我们和其他人一起去了”的电子邮件。 从那里，我发现自己看到了更多的教学设计职位招聘信息，要求应聘者具有编码知识，所以我决定学习它。 我有一篇博客文章更深入地探讨了这个故事，所以我强烈建议您有机会阅读它。 现在，让我对技术写作特别感兴趣的是，在过去的一年里，我一直在写很多关于我的编码之旅的博客，并为开源项目做出了与文档相关的贡献，所以我决定使之成为我的职业道路。

迈克尔：我注意到你喜欢写关于开源的文章！ 您对开源有什么兴趣？您是否正在关注或希望人们了解任何特定的开源项目？

克里斯汀：协作和包容性。 当您刚刚开始编码之旅时，可能很难找到支持和欢迎的技术人员社区，我感谢开源社区的欢迎并帮助我在旅程中成长。

就我关注的开源项目而言，我是EddieHub的超级粉丝。 这是我开始开源之旅时参与的第一个开源项目。 他们强调在编写代码之前进行协作，并且对非技术和技术背景的人开放，因此我强烈推荐给初学者。

包容性开源社区 - 协作第一，代码第二！ 加入我们的 GitHub 组织 👇 - EddieHub

我也喜欢LinksHub 。 这是我的同事Rupali Haldiya ( @rupali_codes ) 创建的一个项目，旨在提供资源以帮助来自不同背景的开发人员学习新技能。 现在，我们正在参加今年的GirlScript Summer of Code ，因此我一直在帮助该计划中的人们做出他们的第一个开源贡献。 作为 LinksHub 的维护者并帮助 GSSoC 成员对我来说是一次很好的学习经历，并且扩展了我对开源的看法。


克里斯汀：很长一段时间以来，我从没想过学习编码是可能的，因为我从未见过女性担任技术职位。 如果我在十几岁和大学期间接触过像Girls Who Code这样的组织，我就会更有信心学习如何编码并更早地从事技术写作。 因此，我在女性历史月期间创建了 Twitter 帖子，承认 womxn 在科技领域的地位，启动了开源皇后区，并维护了一个开源存储库，提供了与Amanda Martin ( @amandamartindev ) 一起创建的 womxn 开源项目的链接，让年轻的womxn 不会像我一样经历冒名顶替综合症的感觉。

这是一份动态文件，可以作为寻找人员的资源，包括女性、跨性别者、非二元性别者和其他代表性不足的性别领导开源项目和公司、担任维护者或以其他方式支持开源。

该资源是对Christine Belzie发起的题为“所有女性拥有的开源项目在哪里”的讨论帖子的回应。 在寻找一些资源来发送 Christine 时，我找不到任何彻底的或当前维护的东西，因此决定开始这个项目。 我们希望这个资源会变得如此庞大，以至于我们必须找到一个更好的解决方案来容纳所有令人惊叹的资源。

欢迎并鼓励为此资源做出贡献。

单击CONTRIBUTING.md以了解如何提交资源。

资料库

通过这个平台，我学到了很多关于重新调整内容用途和使用 Markdown 的知识。 几个月后，我决定成为一名版主，因为我想回馈帮助我成长的社区，并帮助扩大其他博主的声音，尤其是那些不太出名且来自边缘化背景的博主。

迈克尔：作为一名在软件开发行业工作的非技术人员，我偶尔会有冒名顶替综合症的感觉。 您是否遇到过同样的问题？如果遇到过，您是如何应对的？

最终帮助我应对这些感受的是采用耐克的口号：“Just do it!” 我知道这听起来很难，但有时候，你只需要忽略那些在你脑海中盘旋的消极想法。 知道你有技能可以提供，并且只是工作。

包起来

谢谢大家阅读。 请继续关注本系列未来的模组访谈！



原文:

In this series, we shine a spotlight on the different DEV moderators — Trusted Members and Tag Mods — who help to make DEV a kind, helpful place. Aside from spreading good vibes and helping fellow community members, these folks also assist us with removing spam and keeping posts well organized by adding and removing tags as necessary amongst other things.

If you want to learn more about what these awesome folks do, I recommend checking out our Trusted Member and Tag Moderation guides. There is information about how to apply in both guides if you're interested in joining up as a moderator.

Introducing Christine Belzie 🙌

This month, we're featuring Christine Belzie, who assists us as a Trusted Member and Tag Moderator for #beginners, #opensource, and #writing. Christine's unique pathway into the industry is not as a software developer, but rather a technical writer. Her goals around championing inclusivity and diversity in the open source world very much align with our values here at DEV and make her an excellent addition to our mod team. I had the pleasure of interviewing Christine asynchronously recently and am siked to share it with you all here. Thank you, Christine!

The Interview

Let's jump right into my questions and Christine's answers!

Michael Tharrington: Can you talk a bit about how you got interested in tech as a non-developer and your goals to become a technical writer for the dev industry?

Christine Belzie: A couple of years ago, I was interviewed for an Instructional Designer position for an EdTech company. Everything went pretty smoothly until I was asked if I had a basic knowledge of HTML. I took a deep breath, and told the interviewer “No”. A couple of days later, I received the classic “We went with someone else” email. From there, I found myself seeing more Instructional Design job postings requesting candidates with coding knowledge, so I decided to learn it. I have a blog post that delves more into the story, so I highly recommend reading it when you get the chance. Now, what got me interested in tech writing, in particular, is the fact that I've been doing a lot of blogging about my coding journey and making docs-related contributions to open-source projects in the past year, so I decided to make that my career path.

Michael: I’ve noticed that you like to write about open source! What interests you in open source and are there any particular open source projects that you’re following or would like folks to know about?

Christine: Collaboration and inclusivity. When you’re just starting your coding journey, it can be difficult to find a community of people in technology who are supportive and welcoming, and I thank the open-source community for being welcoming and helping me grow in my journey.

As far as open-source projects that I follow, I am a huge fan of EddieHub. It was the very first open-source project that I contributed to when I started my open-source journey. They have an emphasis on collaboration before code and are open to people from non-tech and tech backgrounds, so I highly recommend it for beginners.

Community of inclusive Open Source people - Collaboration 1st, Code 2nd! Join our GitHub Org 👇 - EddieHub

I also love LinksHub. It is a project that my colleague, Rupali Haldiya (@rupali_codes), created to provide resources to help developers from all backgrounds learn new skills. Right now, we’re participating in this year’s GirlScript Summer of Code, so I have been helping people in that program with making their first open-source contributions. Being a maintainer for LinksHub and helping GSSoC members has been a great learning experience for me and has expanded my perspective on open source.


Michael: I saw on Twitter that you list in your bio: “Championing #inclusivity in coding spaces”… Would you please talk about why you believe diversity and representation are so important for us to focus on in the tech industry?

Christine: For a long time, I never thought that learning to code was possible because I have never seen women in tech roles. If I had been exposed to organizations like Girls Who Code during my teenage years and college, I would have had more confidence to learn how to code and pursued tech writing earlier. So, I have created Twitter posts acknowledging womxn in tech during Women’s History Month, started Open Source Queens, and maintain an open-source repository that provides links to womxn-founded open-source projects with Amanda Martin (@amandamartindev), so that young womxn won’t experience the same feelings of imposter syndrome as I did.

This is a living document that can serve as a resource for finding people including those identifying as women, transgender people, nonbinary people, and other underrepresented genders leading open source projects and companies, serving as maintainers, or otherwise supporting Open Source.

This resource began in reponse to a discussion post started by Christine Belzie titled Where are all the women owned Open Source projects. In looking for some resources to send Christine, I was unable to find anything thorough or currently maintained and decided to start this project. It is our hope that this resource will get so large that we will have to find a better solution to hold all the amazing resources.

Contributions to this resource are welcome and encouraged.

Click on the CONTRIBUTING.md to learn how to submit resources.

Repositories

Alison Gianotto aka "snipe" - Snipe-IT

bashbunni - Devrel @ Charm and other projects like…

Michael How did you first discover DEV and what encouraged you to become a moderator for the community?

Christine: I have been trying to find a way to grow my audience on Hashnode and learned that DEV.to is another platform where new tech bloggers post their content, so I decided to join the community. I have learned a lot about repurposing content and working with Markdown through this platform. After a couple of months, I decided to become a moderator because I wanted to give back to the community that helped me grow and help amplify the voices of other bloggers, especially those who are not as well known and come from marginalized backgrounds.

Michael: As a fellow non-technical person working in the software dev industry, I occasionally have feelings of imposter syndrome. Do you ever get hit with this same issue and if so, how do you cope?

Christine: I have had moments of imposter syndrome, especially when I first started my coding journey. What has ultimately helped me cope with these feelings is adopting Nike’s slogan: 'Just do it!' I know that sounds difficult, but sometimes, you just have to ignore those negative thoughts that swirl in your head. Know that you have skills to offer, and just work.

Wrap up

Thank y'all for reading. Stay tuned for future mod interviews in this series!
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 打下坚实的基础：掌握数据分析职业的基本技术 | Building a Strong Foundation: Mastering Essential Technologies for Data Analysis Careers
item.description:
介绍

在当今数据驱动的世界中，对熟练数据分析师的需求正在飙升。 各行各业的公司都依赖数据分析来获得洞察力、做出明智的决策并推动业务增长。

要开始成功的数据分析职业，在行业中使用的技术和工具方面打下坚实的基础至关重要。
在本文中，我们将探索有抱负的数据分析师应该学习的最重要的技术，以便在他们的职业生涯中脱颖而出。

由于其简单性、多功能性和广泛的库生态系统，Python 已成为数据分析的首选编程语言。

掌握 Python 编程是使数据分析师能够高效地处理和分析数据的一项基本技能。

它提供了一个多维数组对象和一组数学函数来高效地执行数值计算。

使用 Pandas 进行探索性数据分析 (EDA)。

它提供直观的数据结构，例如 DataFrame，使分析师能够高效地清理、转换和探索数据。

掌握 Pandas 可以让分析师从数据中获得洞察力，并为进一步分析做好准备。

使用 Matplotlib 和 Seaborn 进行数据可视化。

有效的数据可视化对于传达数据中的见解和模式至关重要。

数据分析师应该学会利用这些库来创建引人注目的数据可视化表示。

统计和统计模型。

对统计学的扎实理解对于数据分析师从数据中得出准确的结论至关重要。

概率、假设检验、回归分析和方差分析等概念在分析和解释数据中起着至关重要的作用。

精通统计技术和模型使分析师具备必要的技能，以发现有意义的见解并做出数据驱动的决策。

使用不同类型的数据集。

数据分析师会遇到各种类型的数据集，包括结构化、非结构化和半结构化数据。
熟悉不同的数据格式，例如 CSV、JSON 和 XML，对于提取和操作数据很重要。

此外，了解数据存储系统（例如关系数据库和 NoSQL 数据库）可以让分析师有效地处理不同的数据源。

结构化查询语言 (SQL)。

精通 SQL 使分析师能够有效地提取、转换和分析存储在数据库中的数据。

了解 SQL 语法、数据库设计原则和高级查询技术使分析师能够处理大型数据集并执行复杂的数据转换。

使用 Tableau 或 PowerBI 讲述数据故事。

数据讲故事是一种以引人注目且有意义的方式传达见解和发现的艺术。

学习使用这些工具创建引人入胜的视觉叙事，可以增强向技术和非技术利益相关者有效传达数据见解的能力。

商业头脑和解决问题。

数据分析不仅仅是处理数字； 它需要对业务目标和问题有深刻的理解。

培养强大的商业头脑可以让数据分析师将他们的分析与组织的目标保持一致，并提出可行的建议。 此外，磨练解决问题的能力使分析师能够应对复杂的数据挑战并找到创新的解决方案。

机器学习基础知识和预测分析。

机器学习是一个快速发展的领域，它使数据分析师能够构建预测模型并进行数据驱动的预测。 了解机器学习算法、模型评估技术和特征工程的基础知识可以增强从数据中提取有意义的模式和预测的能力。 熟悉 Scikit-learn 或 TensorFlow 等库可为机器学习任务奠定坚实的基础。

时间序列分析与预测。

在金融、销售和股票市场分析等领域普遍存在的时间序列数据需要专门的技术来进行分析和预测。

学习 ARIMA、指数平滑和 Prophet 等时间序列分析方法可为数据分析师提供工具，以发现趋势、季节性，并在时间相关的数据集中做出准确的预测。

商业案例研究与分析。

研究真实世界的商业案例研究和分析数据驱动的场景对于有抱负的数据分析师来说是一项非常宝贵的练习。 从事实际项目可以让分析师在现实环境中应用他们的技术技能，获得实践经验，并从真正的挑战和解决方案中学习。

结论：掌握本文概述的基本技术和概念为数据分析事业的成功奠定了坚实的基础。 通过获得这些技能并不断学习和适应新兴技术，有抱负的数据分析师可以在数据驱动的世界中找到令人兴奋的机会。



原文:

Introduction

In today's data-driven world, the demand for skilled data analysts is soaring. Companies across industries rely on data analysis to gain insights, make informed decisions, and drive business growth.

To embark on a successful data analysis career, it is crucial to acquire a solid foundation in the technologies and tools used in the industry.
In this article, we will explore the most important technologies that aspiring data analysts should learn to excel in their careers.

1). Python Programming.

Python has emerged as the go-to programming language for data analysis due to its simplicity, versatility, and extensive library ecosystem.

Python provides a wide range of libraries and frameworks, such as Pandas, NumPy, and Scikit-learn, that are specifically designed for data manipulation, analysis, and machine learning tasks.

Mastering Python programming is a fundamental skill that enables data analysts to efficiently process and analyze data.

2).Understanding NumPy.

NumPy is a powerful library for scientific computing in Python. It provides a multidimensional array object and a collection of mathematical functions to efficiently perform numerical computations.

NumPy's array operations and functions are optimized for performance, making it an essential tool for handling large datasets and performing complex calculations in data analysis.

3). Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) with Pandas.

Pandas is a widely used Python library for data manipulation and analysis. It offers intuitive data structures, such as DataFrame, that enable analysts to clean, transform, and explore data efficiently.

Pandas provides essential functionalities for data wrangling, handling missing values, grouping, and aggregation. Mastering Pandas allows analysts to gain insights from data and prepare it for further analysis.

**4). Data Visualization with Matplotlib and Seaborn.

Effective data visualization is crucial for communicating insights and patterns in data.

Matplotlib and Seaborn are popular Python libraries for creating visually appealing and informative plots, charts, and graphs. Matplotlib provides a flexible and customizable plotting interface, while Seaborn offers high-level statistical visualization capabilities.

Data analysts should learn to leverage these libraries to create compelling visual representations of data.

5). Statistics and Statistical Models.

A solid understanding of statistics is essential for data analysts to draw accurate conclusions from data.

Concepts such as probability, hypothesis testing, regression analysis, and ANOVA play a vital role in analyzing and interpreting data.

Proficiency in statistical techniques and models equips analysts with the necessary skills to uncover meaningful insights and make data-driven decisions.

6). Working with Different Types of Datasets.

Data analysts encounter various types of datasets, including structured, unstructured, and semi-structured data.
Familiarity with different data formats, such as CSV, JSON, and XML, is important for extracting and manipulating data.

Additionally, understanding data storage systems, such as relational databases and NoSQL databases, allows analysts to efficiently work with different data sources.

7). Structured Query Language (SQL).

SQL is a standard language for managing and querying relational databases.
Proficiency in SQL enables analysts to extract, transform, and analyze data stored in databases effectively.

Understanding SQL syntax, database design principles, and advanced querying techniques empowers analysts to handle large datasets and perform complex data transformations.

8). Data Storytelling with Tableau or PowerBI.

Data storytelling is the art of conveying insights and findings in a compelling and meaningful way.

Tableau and PowerBI are popular data visualization tools that provide interactive dashboards and storytelling capabilities. Learning to create engaging visual narratives using these tools enhances the ability to communicate data insights effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

9). Business Acumen and Problem Solving.

Data analysis is not solely about crunching numbers; it requires a deep understanding of business objectives and problems.

Developing strong business acumen allows data analysts to align their analysis with the organization's goals and make actionable recommendations. Additionally, honing problem-solving skills enables analysts to tackle complex data challenges and find innovative solutions.

10). Machine Learning Basics & Predictive Analytics.

Machine learning is a rapidly evolving field that empowers data analysts to build predictive models and make data-driven forecasts. Understanding the basics of machine learning algorithms, model evaluation techniques, and feature engineering enhances the ability to extract meaningful patterns and predictions from data. Familiarity with libraries like Scikit-learn or TensorFlow provides a solid foundation for machine learning tasks.

11). Time Series Analysis & Forecasting.

Time series data, which is prevalent in areas such as finance, sales, and stock market analysis, requires specialized techniques for analysis and forecasting.

Learning time series analysis methods like ARIMA, Exponential Smoothing, and Prophet equips data analysts with the tools to uncover trends, seasonality, and make accurate predictions in time-dependent datasets.

12). Business Case Studies & Analysis.

Studying real-world business case studies and analyzing data-driven scenarios is an invaluable exercise for aspiring data analysts. Working on practical projects allows analysts to apply their technical skills in a realistic context, gain hands-on experience, and learn from real challenges and solutions.

Conclusion: Mastering the essential technologies and concepts outlined in this article provides a solid foundation for a successful career in data analysis. Python programming, statistical knowledge, data manipulation, visualization skills, and the ability to derive actionable insights are all key components for becoming a proficient data analyst.

By acquiring these skills and continuously learning and adapting to emerging technologies, aspiring data analysts can position themselves for exciting opportunities in a data-driven world.
item.author: grayhat

item.title: 为什么您需要为多应用程序业务使用集中式身份系统 | Why you need a centralized identity system for a multi-app business
item.description:
构建身份系统是一项复杂的任务，需要对业务模型和要求进行全面评估。 随着业务的发展壮大，构建多个应用程序成为自然而然的一步。 优先考虑身份基础设施对于多应用程序业务的成功至关重要。

使用 Logto 构建多应用业务身份

在本文中，让我们探讨为多应用业务构建可靠身份系统所涉及的最佳实践和细节，并深入探讨应考虑的因素。

什么是多应用业务，哪些类型的产品可以从该策略中受益？

多平台

最简单的场景是拥有 Web、移动和本机（iOS 或 Android）版本的业务，其中身份系统是统一的。 建议将每个版本标记为单独的应用程序，因为它们是向客户提供的不同产品。 这使您能够：

  1. 通过分析单个应用程序的使用行为和审核日志来增强增长和安全性。
  2. 按渠道确定采集性能和流量。
  3. 优先考虑每个应用程序的开发和分发策略。

多样化的产品线或一套服务

另一种情况是，一家公司希望在其所有产品中保持统一的品牌标识，同时允许每种产品都有其独特的应用程序来迎合不同的客户群。 让我们举一个真实世界的例子。

它提供各种应用程序，包括 Spotify for Podcasters、Spotify Stations 和 Spotify Connect，以及它的主要应用程序，所有这些应用程序都具有通用的登录体验和一个用户身份系统。

同样，健康和健身公司可能会为瑜伽、跑步和举重创建单独的应用程序，每个应用程序都具有独特的功能和内容以及用户界面。

多边市场

另一个典型的场景是多边商业模式，在这种模式下，公司通过促进两个或多个客户群体之间的互动来创造价值。

例如，Uber 和 Airbnb 分别促进了乘客与司机或旅行者与房东之间的互动。 为了实现这种供需交换，公司可能会开发多种产品。

市场双方的统一用户系统可以提供多种优势。 用户可以享受跨各种应用程序的无缝体验，避免设置单独帐户的不便。 此外，它还适用于一个段移动到另一个段的情况。

访问控制和高级授权功能可以进一步增强这种最佳实践。

为什么集中式身份系统很重要，没有这样的系统有什么风险？

对于常见问题空间或域中的多应用程序业务策略，请考虑实施集中式身份系统。

在需要时，它有助于隔离和分发身份。 另一方面，如果一开始就创建了多个身份，合并和删除不需要的用户身份可能是一项繁琐且令人沮丧的工作，对任何类型的业务都构成高风险：

  • 多个账户：缺乏统一的用户系统可能导致用户不得不为每个应用程序创建和管理多个账户，从而导致碎片化和令人沮丧的用户体验。 例如，用户可能会忘记他们的登录凭据，从而导致帐户丢失并增加企业的支持负担。

  • 开发复杂性和成本增加：开发人员在为每个应用程序构建和维护不同的用户身份验证和授权系统时可能会遇到挑战，从而导致开发复杂性和成本增加。 实施不当还可能导致应用程序之间存在差异，从而给最终客户带来相当大的风险。

  • 用户访问管理挑战：缺乏集中式用户系统会给管理跨多个应用程序的用户访问和权限带来挑战，需要在各种身份系统中建立不同的授权策略，并需要额外努力来识别正确的用户。

  • 合规性和隐私问题：为了遵守法规并解决隐私问题，企业必须妥善管理用户身份和数据。 一个非中心化的身份系统可能会造成额外的努力或障碍来完成及时删除用户数据等任务，从而导致潜在的处罚和罚款。

  • 安全漏洞：非集中式身份系统中的共享密码和弱身份验证机制可能会产生影响多个应用程序的安全漏洞。 它可以减少用户需要与各个应用共享的个人信息。

要启用集中式身份系统，请执行以下操作

选择身份和架构匹配的解决方案

市场上有许多可用的身份解决方案，但有些可能过于复杂或缺乏可扩展性和灵活性。 选择身份管理系统时，重要的是要考虑其架构以及它如何适应您的多应用场景。

定义访问控制

定义访问控制对于确保身份系统的安全性至关重要。 访问控制指定哪些用户有权跨各种应用程序访问特定资源，以及在什么级别。 该定义能够保护敏感数据并建立适应性授权机制。

设计有凝聚力的登录体验

登录流程应该是通用的，涵盖所有产品，所使用的身份验证方法应该在所有应用程序中无缝运行。 在不同应用程序中优先考虑一致的品牌和外观至关重要，因为不这样做可能会削弱客户的信任。

不要忽视可扩展性

您的身份系统应该是可扩展的以适应未来的增长。 考虑未来可能增加的用户和应用程序的数量，并选择能够适应这些变化的解决方案。

使用 Logto 进行多应用业务标识

我们的原创架构和互补功能可为各种规模的企业提供支持。

多应用架构

用户能够创建一个可以托管多个应用程序的单个租户，从而在管理用户和跨应用程序跟踪日志方面提供灵活性。

通用登录体验

登录体验适用于租户内的所有应用程序，无需将身份验证和品牌设置复制或传输到各个应用程序。

集中用户管理

基于角色的访问控制

立即试用 Logto



原文:

Building an identity system is a complex task that demands a thorough evaluation of business models and requirements. As businesses grow and expand, building multiple apps becomes a natural step. Prioritizing identity infrastructure is critical to success in a multi-app business.

Use Logto to build multi-app business identities

In this article, let's explore the best practices and details involved in building a reliable identity system for a multi-app business and dive deep into the factors which should be considered.

What is a multi-app business, and which types of products can benefit from this strategy?

Multi-platform

The simplest scenario is a business with the web, mobile, and native (iOS or Android) versions, where the identity system is unified. It is recommended to label each version as a separate app since they are distinct offerings to customers. This enables you to:

  1. Enhance growth and security by analyzing usage behaviors and audit logs for individual applications.
  2. Determine acquisition performance and traffic by channel.
  3. Prioritize development and distribution strategies per application.

Diverse product lines or a suite of services

Another scenario is when a company wants to maintain a uniform brand identity across all its products while allowing each product to have its distinct app to cater to different customer segments. Let’s take a real-world example.

Spotify is an excellent example of this approach. It offers various apps, including Spotify for Podcasters, Spotify Stations, and Spotify Connect, in addition to its primary app, all with a universal sign-in experience and one user identity system.

Similarly, a health and fitness company may create separate apps for yoga, running, and weightlifting, each with unique features and content, and user interfaces.

Multi-sided marketplace

Another typical scenario is a multi-sided business model, where a company creates value by facilitating interactions between two or more groups of customers.

For example, Uber and Airbnb facilitate interactions between riders and drivers or travelers and hosts, respectively. To enable this demand-supply exchange, companies may develop multiple products.

A unified user system for both sides of a marketplace can offer several advantages. Users can enjoy a seamless experience across various applications and avoid the inconvenience of setting up separate accounts. Furthermore, it accommodates scenarios where one segment moves to another.

Access controls and advanced authorization functionalities can further enhance this best practice.

Why is a centralized identity system important and what are the risks of not having one?

🔥 Centralized identity systems are crucial for fast-growing businesses as they offer better safety and flexibility and the foundation to be enterprise-ready if you are a SaaS product！

For a multi-app business strategy within a common problem space or domain, consider implementing a centralized identity system.

It can be beneficial in isolating and distributing identities when needed. On the other hand, merging and deleting unwanted user identities can be a cumbersome and frustrating task if multiple identities were created at the beginning, posing a high risk for any type of business：

  • Multiple accounts: A lack of a unified user system can result in users having to create and manage multiple accounts for each app, causing a fragmented and frustrating user experience. For example, users may forget their login credentials, leading to lost accounts and a higher support burden for the business.

  • Increased development complexity and cost: Developers may encounter challenges in constructing and upholding distinct user authentication and authorization systems for each application, resulting in augmented development complexity and cost. Improper implementation could also lead to disparities between apps, posing a considerable risk to end customers.

  • User access management challenges: Lack of a centralized user system can create challenges in managing user access and permissions across multiple applications, requiring establishing different authorization policies in various identity systems and extra effort to identify the right users.

  • Compliance and privacy concerns: To comply with regulations and address privacy concerns, businesses must properly manage user identities and data. An uncentralized identity system can create an extra layer of effort or obstacle to achieve tasks such as the timely deletion of user data, leading to potential penalties and fines.

  • Security vulnerabilities: Shared passwords and weak authentication mechanisms in an uncentralized identity system can create security vulnerabilities that impact multiple apps. It can reduce the personal information users need to share with individual apps.

To enable a centralized identity system, do these

Choose an identity-and-architecture fit solution

There are many identity solutions available in the market, but some can be too complex or lack scalability and flexibility. When choosing an identity management system, it's important to consider its architecture and how it fits with your multi-app scenario.

Define access control

Defining access control is crucial in ensuring the security of the identity system. Access control specifies which users have permission to access particular resources, and at what level, across various applications. This definition enables the protection of sensitive data and establishes an adaptable authorization mechanism.

Design a cohesive sign-in experience

The sign-in flow should be universal, covering all products, and the authentication methods utilized should function seamlessly across all applications. It is crucial to prioritize the consistent branding and appearance across different apps, as failure to do so may erode customer trust.

Don't overlook scalability

Your identity system should be scalable to accommodate future growth. Consider the number of users and applications that may be added in the future, and choose a solution that can accommodate these changes.

Use Logto for multi-app business identity

Logto is ideal for multi-app businesses, supporting growth from startup to corporate. Our original architecture and complementary features empower businesses of all sizes.

Multi-app architecture

Users are able to create a single tenant which can host multiple apps, offering flexibility in managing users and tracking logs across the apps.

Universal sign-in experience

The sign-in experience applies to all apps within a tenant, eliminating the need to duplicate or transfer authentication and branding settings to individual apps.

Centralized user management

Logto offers a centralized user pool to manage all tenant users, along with an advanced search API for more targeted management based on specific needs.

Role-based access control

Logto provides a simple yet effective RBAC support, making it an excellent complement to multi-app scenarios and enhancing the security of complex businesses with multiple applications.

Try Logto today
item.author: fleuraly

item.title: 2023 年计算机科学专业毕业后要考虑的最佳职业选择 | Best Career Options to Consider after Graduating in Computer Science in 2023
item.description:
对于年轻有希望的人来说，一个流行的职业选择一直是计算机工程。 这主要是由于该领域的广泛专业化，这为计算机工程师提供了许多就业选择。 然而，对于许多人来说，计算机工程领域的大量就业选择可能令人生畏。 完成学业后，许多人在寻找计算机工程师的工作时感到困惑。

事实上，只有一个难题是那些还在为自己的职业生涯犹豫不决的学生必须不断回答的问题：

问题“计算机工程之后做什么？”

这个问题没有单一的回答，是我们的回答。 这就是使计算机工程如此美丽的原因。 在计算机工程之后，有许多就业机会，而不仅仅是一个。 从各种计算机工程职业中进行选择。 获得计算机工程学士学位后，将有许多选择。
你可以根据提供的各种计算机工程领域选择你想做的工作。 下面列出了与计算机工程的一些主要类别相关的工作。

对于那些寻求进一步教育的人来说，最好的选择是计算机工程专业的 M.Tech/MS。 通常，M.Tech 课程持续两年，而 MS 课程持续三年。 获得 M.Tech./M.Sc. 学位后，您有两种选择。 计算机工程专业：您可以申请工作或攻读博士学位。 在研究中。

博士获得 M.Tech 后的课程。 对于有兴趣研究该地区的学生来说，这是一个很好的选择。 博士课程通常持续五年，包括两年的课程学习和至少三年的研究。

双学位（Ph.D. and M.Tech.） - 这接近于博士学位。 唯一的区别是双学位需要 M.Tech。 和博士学位

商业分析、数据科学和机器学习的研究生课程。 当前行业的热门趋势包括数据科学及其相关技术，包括人工智能、机器学习和深度学习。 此外，业务分析是与大数据和数据科学同步发展的新领域。 对于那些想在数据科学和大数据领域工作的人，建议获得数据科学或商业分析的研究生学位。 这是同一课程的推荐课程-计算机科学硕士学位。

数据科学家是数据科学领域的专家。 他们的主要重点是从复杂的数据中提取有用的信息。 数据科学家从各种外部来源收集信息，包括社交媒体、网站、博客和其他在线内容。 他们整理、整理和处理这些数据，以便他们可以进一步分析它并找到任何潜在的模式。 这使他们能够理解他们所掌握的数据如何解决组织当前的业务困难。

数据科学家和分析师一起深入研究数据，收集有用的见解，这些见解可用于解决现实世界商业应用程序中的问题。 在处理涉及大量复杂业务数据的计划时，数据科学家经常处于领先地位（很像项目经理）。 数据科学家通常对数学、统计学、工程学、计算机科学和工程学有深入的了解。 这里推荐同样的课程——Scaler Data Science and Machine learning 。

设计、开发、实施和维护网络和数据传输系统的专业人员被称为计算机网络架构师。 这包括内联网、外联网以及局域网和广域网。 此外，他们还负责更新硬件（如适配器和路由器）和软件（如网络驱动程序）。 计算机网络架构师通常与公司的首席技术官合作，以预见新网络最有用的组织领域。

计算机网络架构师需要牢牢掌握公司的业务战略，以便在适当的位置构建和部署网络。 为了保护系统免受缺陷，他们还必须安排安全更新和其他补救措施的应用。 此外，计算机网络架构师还必须研究新的网络技术，以正确分析当前的数据流量并预测流量增长如何影响网络。 这是相同的推荐课程 - Computer Networks Foundations 。

为企业构建、测试和维护可扩展的大数据生态系统是大数据工程师的职责，以便数据科学家可以在可靠和有效的数据平台上运行他们的算法。 大数据工程师通常与数据架构师、数据分析师和数据科学家密切合作，以协助组织从规模庞大且复杂的数据集中获得有价值的见解，然后这些数据可用于为业务层面的决策提供信息。 在计算机工程之后，最好的职业道路之一是大数据。

顾名思义，大数据工程师主要关注大数据生态系统、工具和技术。 为了提高数据库的有效性，他们必须升级、调试和优化大数据系统和软件。 大数据工程师通常需要几年的业务工作经验才能使用 Hadoop 和其他大数据框架、基于 SQL 的数据库，以及众所周知的数据 API 和 ETL 工具。 这里推荐同样的课程——大数据专业。

机器学习工程师是高技能的 ML 专业人员，他们专注于创建智能机器和系统，这些机器和系统可以从经验中学习并执行人类执行的活动，而几乎不需要人工干预。 他们开发了尖端的 ML 算法，可以指导计算机如何执行特定的工作，而无需对其进行明确的编程。 此外，机器学习工程师还受雇创建尖端的 ML 和深度学习系统，并进行大量 ML 测试和实验。

机器学习工程师是当今需求量最大的职业之一。 然而，职位描述要求精通计算机科学、统计学和数学。 通过检查机器学习工程师收到的报酬类型，您可以确定对他们服务的需求水平。 机器学习工程师必须透彻理解计算机科学的基本概念，例如数据结构、算法和计算机体系结构。 不用说，他们必须是精通各种语言的高技能程序员，包括 Python、R、Java、C、Ruby、Perl、Scala 等。这里推荐同样的课程——机器学习专业化。

顾名思义，区块链开发人员/工程师是专注于区块链技术的软件专业人员。 区块链应用程序的整个生命周期，从研究和分析到设计和实施，都由区块链开发人员和工程师处理和监督。

他们结合了多种区块链编程语言来为区块链系统（Simplicity、Solidity、Rholang、JavaScript、C++ 等）开发复杂的用户界面、功能和架构。 区块链开发人员和工程师为区块链应用程序和解决方案建立必要的基础设施和关键的安全措施，以保护系统免受网络攻击并创建最佳实践。

区块链开发人员和工程师必须透彻了解 Web 开发、智能合约创建、密码学、数据结构和区块链架构。 这是相同的推荐课程 -区块链：基础和用例。

数据库管理员在行业中起着至关重要的作用，因为他们的主要职责是确保公司数据库每天正常运行。 他们监督对现有软件项目所做的调整、数据库的维护、数据库访问的管理以及数据库软件采购的监控和跟踪。

为了保护和维护机密数据的完整性和隐私，数据库管理员还负责业务数据库的质量控制和功能。

数据库管理员需要非常有条理和善于分析。 他们必须愿意跟上最新的技术进步，并对 IT 的业务需求有深入的了解。 处理大量数据的IT公司、管理咨询公司、金融机构、学术机构，甚至政府机构都会聘请数据库管理员。 这是相同的推荐课程-数据库管理员（DBA）| 全栈开发人员的主要任务包括编码和编程。 他们遵循公认的 HTML/CSS 最佳实践，并根据客户的要求和要求设计和开发网站。 他们还必须跟上必要的软件文档。 全栈开发人员需要精通图形设计和计算机编程，因为这个职位强调用户体验和功能。

由于数字和电子商务领域企业之间的激烈竞争，它们的需求量仍然很大。 全栈工程师需要客户端元素、服务器端语言、Web 应用程序框架和数据库管理系统的技术知识。 这里推荐同样的课程——哈佛Web开发者专业证书。

管理和部署组织的计算机和信息技术 (IT) 系统的业务主管被称为信息系统经理。 它们充当组织最高管理层和 IT 专业人员之间的联络点。 他们确保组织使用的所有计算机系统、软件事件和其他硬件/软件元素安全高效地运行。

其他 IT 专家，如计算机工程师、系统分析师、数据安全专家、技术支持专家等，由信息系统经理监督和管理。

信息系统经理有很好的工作前景，因为大多数雇主招聘他们是为了提高员工生产力和公司运营。 信息系统经理必须具备软技能，包括领导能力、沟通能力、项目管理、时间管理、客户服务以及强大的组织和管理技能。 这是管理信息系统中相同的证书课程的推荐课程。

继计算机工程之后，计算机系统分析是一条重要的专业道路，专注于分析组织当前的计算机系统、设计信息系统和业务运营，以开发更具创造性、更有效和更具生产力的解决方案。 计算机系统分析师有助于弥合信息技术 (IT) 和商业世界，因为他们对两者都有深入的了解。

为了提高计算机系统的有效性和性能，他们在设计系统时使用各种策略并进行密集测试以识别数据中的任何隐藏模式。

公司可能会聘请专业分析师，例如系统架构师和软件质量保证 (QA) 分析师，以及程序员分析师，此外还会聘请计算机系统分析师作为设计新系统或改进现有系统的通用分析师。 这里推荐同样的课程—— 系统分析证书课程。

结论

这些是您可以从计算机工程专业中选择的 12 大职业。 由于该行业对各种专业选择的巨大需求，他们都提供中等到非常高的年薪。

如果您想了解更多关于计算机工程之后的职业道路，请根据您的兴趣与我们的一位职业顾问交谈。 他们可以提供有关当前市场趋势、各种行业的潜在增长等方面的信息。



原文:

A popular career choice for young hopefuls has always been computer engineering. This is primarily due to the field's wide range of specializations, which gives computer engineers many employment options. However, for many, the sheer amount of employment options in computer engineering might be intimidating. after completing their education, many people become confused while looking for work as computer engineers.

In reality, there is just one difficult issue that students who are still deciding what they want in their professional lives must constantly answer:

The question "What to do after computer engineering?"

There is no single response to this question, is our response. And that's what makes computer engineering so beautiful. After computer engineering, there are numerous employment opportunities rather than just one. Choose from a variety of computer engineering careers. After receiving your Bachelor's degree in computer engineering, many alternatives will be available.
You can select the job you want to do based on the various computer engineering fields offered. The jobs connected to some of the main categories of computer engineering are listed below.

1. MS in CS

The greatest option for those seeking to further their education is the M.Tech/M.S. in Computer Engineering. Typically, M.Tech programs last two years, while M.S. courses last three years. You have two options after earning your M.Tech./M.Sc. in computer engineering: you can either apply for jobs or pursue a Ph.D. in research.

Ph.D. - You can immediately enroll in a Ph.D. program after earning your M.Tech. This is a great option for students interested in researching the area. Doctoral programs typically last five years, including two years of coursework and at least three years of research.

Dual degree (Ph.D. and M.Tech.) - This is close to the Ph.D. The sole distinction is that a dual degree requires an M.Tech. and a Ph.D.

Graduate-level course in business analytics, data science, and machine learning. Current hot trends in the industry include data science and its related technologies, including artificial intelligence, machine learning, and deep learning. Additionally, business analytics is a new field developing concurrently with big data and data science. A postgraduate degree in data science or business analytics is recommended for those who want to work in the data science and big data fields. Here is recommended course for the same - Computer Science MS Degree.

2. Data Scientist

Data Scientists are the experts in the field of data science. Their main focus is on extracting useful information from complicated data. Data scientists collect information from various external sources, including social media, websites, blogs, and other online content. They tidy up, arrange, and process this data so that they can further analyze it and find any underlying patterns. This enables them to comprehend how the data at their disposal can address the organization's current business difficulties.

Together, data scientists and analysts delve deeply into the data to glean useful insights that may be applied to solve problems with real-world commercial applications. Data scientists frequently lead when working on initiatives involving significant amounts of complicated business data (much like a project manager). Data scientists typically have in-depth knowledge of mathematics, statistics, engineering, computer science, and engineering. Here is recommended course for the same - Scaler Data Science and Machine learning.

3. Computer Network Architect

Professionals who design, develop, implement, and maintain networking and data transmission systems are known as computer network architects. This covers intranets, extranets, and local and wide area networks. Additionally, they are in charge of updating hardware (such as adapters and routers) and software (such as network drivers). Computer network architects typically collaborate with the company's chief technology officer to foresee the areas of the organization where new networks will be most useful.

Computer network architects need a firm grasp of a company's business strategy to build and deploy the network in the proper locations. To safeguard the systems from flaws, they must also schedule the application of security updates and other remedies. Furthermore, computer network architects must also investigate new networking technologies to properly analyze present data traffic and predict how traffic growth can influence the network. Here is recommended course for the same - Computer Networks Foundations.

4. Big Data Engineer

Building, testing, and maintaining scalable Big Data ecosystems for enterprises is the responsibility of big data engineers so that data scientists may run their algorithms on reliable and effective data platforms. Big Data Engineers typically collaborate closely with Data Architects, Data Analysts, and Data Scientists to assist organizations in deriving valuable insights from sizable and complicated datasets that can then be utilized to inform decision-making at the business level. After computer engineering, one of the best career paths is in big data.

As the name implies, Big Data Engineers focus largely on Big Data ecosystems, tools, and technologies. To increase the effectiveness of the databases, they must upgrade, debug, and optimize Big Data systems and software. Big Data engineers often need a few years of work experience in the business to work with Hadoop and other Big Data frameworks, SQL-based databases, as well as well-known data APIs and ETL tools. Here is recommended course for the same - Big Data Specialization.

5. Machine Learning Engineer

Machine learning engineers are highly skilled ML professionals who specialize in creating intelligent machines and systems that can learn from experience and carry out activities that humans perform with little to no human intervention. They develop cutting-edge ML algorithms that can instruct computers on how to carry out particular jobs without having to program them to do so explicitly. Additionally, machine learning engineers are employed to create cutting-edge ML and Deep Learning systems and conduct numerous ML tests and experiments.

Engineers in machine learning are among the most in-demand occupations today. However, the job description calls for high proficiency in computer science, statistics, and mathematics. By examining the kind of pay machine learning engineers receive, you may determine the level of demand for their services. The foundational concepts of computer science, such as data structures, algorithms, and computer architecture, must be thoroughly understood by machine learning engineers. It goes without saying that they must be highly skilled programmers fluent in various languages, including Python, R, Java, C, Ruby, Perl, Scala, etc. Here is recommended course for the same - Machine Learning Specialization.

6. Blockchain Developer/Engineer

Blockchain developers/Engineers are software professionals focusing on blockchain technology, as the name suggests. The entire life cycle of a blockchain application, from research and analysis to design and implementation, is handled and supervised by blockchain developers and engineers.

They combine several Blockchain programming languages to develop sophisticated user interfaces, functionality, and architecture for Blockchain systems (Simplicity, Solidity, Rholang, JavaScript, C++, etc.). Blockchain Developers and Engineers set up the necessary infrastructure for Blockchain applications and solutions and crucial security measures to safeguard the systems from cyberattacks and create best practices.

Blockchain developers and engineers must thoroughly understand web development, smart contract creation, cryptography, data structures, and blockchain architecture. Here is recommended course for the same - Blockchain: Foundations and Use Cases.

7. Database Administrator

Database administrators play a crucial part in the industry because their main responsibility is ensuring the corporate database runs well daily. They oversee the adjustments made to existing software items, the database's upkeep, management of database access, and monitoring and tracking of database software purchases.

To safeguard and uphold the integrity and privacy of confidential data, database administrators are also accountable for the quality control and functionality of business databases.

Database administrators need to be extremely well-organized and analytical. They must be willing to keep up with the newest technological advancements and have a solid understanding of the business requirements of IT. IT companies, management consulting firms, financial institutions, academic institutions, and even government agencies that deal with massive amounts of data hire database administrators. Here is recommended course for the same - Database Administrator (DBA) | Developer- MS SQL Server,TSQL.

8. Full Stack Developer

The main tasks of full-stack developers include coding and programming. They adhere to accepted HTML/CSS best practices and design and develop websites following client requests and requirements. They must also keep up with the necessary software documentation. Full Stack Developers need to be skilled in graphic design and computer programming because this position emphasizes user experience and functionality.

They continue to be in great demand because of the fierce competition between businesses in the digital and e-commerce areas. The technical knowledge of client-side elements, server-side languages, web application frameworks, and database management systems is required by full-stack engineers. Here is recommended course for the same - Harvard Web Developer Professional Certificate.

9. Information Systems Manager

Business executives who manage and deploy an organization's computer and information technology (IT) systems are known as information systems managers. They serve as a point of contact between an organization's top management and IT professionals. They ensure that all computer systems, software events, and other hardware/software elements an organization uses run safely and efficiently.

Other IT specialists, such as computer engineers, systems analysts, data security specialists, tech support specialists, and others, are supervised and managed by information systems managers.

Information systems managers have excellent job prospects because most employers recruit them to increase employee productivity and corporate operations. Information systems managers must have soft skills, including leadership, communication, project management, time management, customer service, and strong organizational and managerial skills. Here is recommended course for the same - Certificate Program In Management Information Systems.

10. Computer Systems Analyst

Following computer engineering, computer systems analysis is a prominent professional path focusing on analyzing an organization's current computer systems, designing information systems, and business operations to develop more creative, effective, and productive solutions. Computer systems analysts contribute to bridging the information technology (IT) and business worlds since they have an in-depth understanding of both.

To boost the effectiveness and performance of computer systems, they use a variety of strategies while designing them and intensive testing to identify any hidden patterns in the data.

Companies may hire specialized analysts, such as Systems Architects and Software Quality Assurance (Q.A.) Analysts, and Programmer Analysts, in addition to hiring computer systems analysts as general-purpose analysts who design new systems or improve current ones. Here is recommended course for the same - Systems Analysis Certificate Program.

Conclusion

These are the top 12 careers you can choose from following computer engineering. Due to the tremendous demand for various professional choices within the sector, they all offer moderate to very high yearly salaries.

If you want to learn more about the career path after computer engineering, depending on your interests, speak with one of our career counselors. They can provide information on current market trends, the potential growth of various professions, and more.
item.author: aartiyadav

item.title: 适合远程工作文化的 11 大 Slack 聊天机器人 | Top 11 Slack Chatbots for remote work culture
item.description:
远程工作有很多优势，但工作场所缺乏身体接触会对团队凝聚力和公司文化意识产生严重影响。 通过采取积极措施改善远程工作文化，组织可以为员工创造一个更高效、更令人满意的环境。 一种方法是围绕沟通建立规则和期望。 建立一个时间表，规定远程员工何时需要在线参加重要会议和头脑风暴会议。

允许远程员工通过聊天、视频会议甚至社交媒体相互交流，有助于创造更有活力的思想交流和开放对话。 此外，与管理层建立定期签到可以帮助员工了解团队的最新目标，并有一个论坛来提问。

组织还可以采取措施，在工作场所没有身体接触的情况下营造一种社区意识和友情。 建立虚拟团队建设和社交活动是远程员工更好地了解彼此并在他们之间建立联系的好方法。

引入团队挑战和认可系统也有助于鼓励远程员工之间的协作氛围。

通过自动执行任务、提供信息和促进协作，Slack 聊天机器人可以成为远程工作文化的宝贵补充。

用于远程工作文化的 Slack 机器人

以下是一些可以增强远程工作文化的 Slack 聊天机器人示例：

站立机器人

一个聊天机器人，它通过提示团队成员分享他们的进度、计划和挑战来促进日常站立会议。 它有助于让每个人都了解情况并鼓励透明度和问责制。

琐事

一个聊天机器人，通过随机配对团队成员进行虚拟咖啡聊天或午餐来促进团队凝聚力。 它促进了可能没有机会定期互动的远程同事之间的联系。

阿不思

辰

协助任务和项目管理的聊天机器人。 它使团队成员能够创建和分配任务、设置截止日期并接收提醒，从而帮助保持远程团队井井有条并步入正轨。

波利

一个聊天机器人，可简化在 Slack 中进行调查和投票的过程。 它允许团队快速轻松地收集反馈、做出决策和收集见解。

上师

存储和检索重要信息的知识管理聊天机器人。 它充当常见问题、公司政策和其他资源的存储库，使远程团队成员更容易访问重要信息。

嘿塔科！

一个促进团队内认可和欣赏的聊天机器人。 它允许团队成员互相赠送虚拟炸玉米饼（或其他可定制的代币），以此作为认可他们的贡献和培养积极的远程工作文化的一种方式。

米坎

帮助安排和协调不同时区会议的聊天机器人。 它有助于找到相互可用的时间段，避免安排冲突，并简化会议安排过程。

凯伯

与 Trello、Jira 或 Asana 等项目管理工具集成的聊天机器人，可在 Slack 中提供更新和通知。 它可以帮助远程团队随时了解任务进度和变化，而无需在不同工具之间切换。

参与

一个聊天机器人，使用户能够在 Slack 中创建和进行快速投票或脉搏调查。 它允许团队快速收集反馈或有效地做出团队决策。

考勤机器人

跟踪和管理员工出勤和休假请求的聊天机器人。 它简化了请求休假、跟踪休假余额以及维护团队成员可用性透明记录的过程。

这些 Slack 聊天机器人可以通过自动执行日常任务、促进沟通和协作以及促进团队参与和认可，为建立积极高效的远程工作文化做出贡献。



原文:

Working remotely has many advantages, but the lack of physical contact in the workplace can have serious implications for team cohesion and sense of company culture. By taking proactive steps to improve the remote work culture, organizations can create a more productive and satisfying environment for employees. One way to do this is to establish rules and expectations around communication. Establish a timeline that dictates when remote employees need to be online for critical meetings and brainstorming sessions.

Allowing remote employees to communicate with one another through chat, video conferencing, and even social media can help to create a more vibrant exchange of ideas and open dialogue. Additionally, setting up regular check-ins with management can help employees stay up to date on goals for the team and have a forum to ask questions.

Organizations can also take steps to create a sense of community and camaraderie in the absence of physical contact in the workplace. Establishing virtual teambuilding and social activities can be a great way for remote employees to get to know each other better and build connections between them.

Introducing team challenges and recognition systems can also help to encourage a collaborative atmosphere between remote employees.

Slack chatbots can be valuable additions to remote work culture by automating tasks, providing information, and facilitating collaboration.

Slack Bots for Remote work culture

Here are some examples of Slack chatbots that can enhance remote work culture:

Standup Bot

A chatbot that facilitates daily stand-up meetings by prompting team members to share their progress, plans, and challenges. It helps keep everyone informed and encourages transparency and accountability.

Trivia

A chatbot that promotes team bonding by randomly pairing team members for virtual coffee chats or lunches. It fosters connections between remote colleagues who may not have the opportunity to interact regularly.

Albus

Albus is your AI-powered knowledge bot for Slack Instantly answer your employees questions with a personalized chatbot trained on your content from Google Driveᵀᴹ, Notion and 100 more.

Tatsu

A chatbot that assists with task and project management. It enables team members to create and assign tasks, set due dates, and receive reminders, helping to keep remote teams organized and on track.

Polly

A chatbot that simplifies the process of conducting surveys and polls within Slack. It allows teams to gather feedback, make decisions, and gather insights quickly and easily.

Guru

A knowledge management chatbot that stores and retrieves important information. It acts as a repository for frequently asked questions, company policies, and other resources, making it easier for remote team members to access vital information.

HeyTaco!

A chatbot that promotes recognition and appreciation within the team. It allows team members to give each other virtual tacos (or other customizable tokens) as a way of recognizing their contributions and fostering a positive remote work culture.

Meekan

A chatbot that helps schedule and coordinate meetings across different time zones. It assists in finding mutually available time slots, avoiding scheduling conflicts, and streamlining the meeting scheduling process.

Kyber

A chatbot that integrates with project management tools like Trello, Jira, or Asana to provide updates and notifications within Slack. It helps remote teams stay informed about task progress and changes without needing to switch between different tools.

EngageWith

A chatbot that enables users to create and conduct quick polls or pulse surveys within Slack. It allows teams to gather quick feedback or make group decisions efficiently.

AttendanceBot

A chatbot that tracks and manages employee attendance and time-off requests. It streamlines the process of requesting time off, tracking leave balances, and maintaining a transparent record of team members' availability.

These Slack chatbots can contribute to building a positive and productive remote work culture by automating routine tasks, promoting communication and collaboration, and fostering team engagement and recognition.
item.author: kpawan24

item.title: 响应式网页设计 | Responsive Web Design
item.description:
在 10 多年前创建网站和应用程序时，甚至没有人想到移动 Web 应用程序。 好的屏幕充其量是全高清的，很少有人会使用 24 英寸或 27 英寸的屏幕尺寸。

今天，情况大不相同。 手持设备种类繁多，与几年前相比，其中大多数具有更高的分辨率。

设备屏幕不断发展，分辨率不断提高，不仅如此，现在还有宽屏幕、曲面屏幕和对角线尺寸超过 40 英寸的屏幕。 这一切都带来了一组新的可能性，但也带来了开发人员和测试人员需要解决的一组新问题。 设备碎片化程度越高，就越需要创建响应式网页设计，以在不同的分辨率下正确呈现。

在本指南中，您将找到最热门的响应式网页设计挑战及其解决方案。

视口

它因设备而异，与台式机相比，手机上的尺寸更小。

早期的网页仅在桌面上使用。 然后，人们开始使用平板电脑和手机上网。 此时，固定大小的网页太大而无法适应视口。 为了解决这个问题，这些设备上的浏览器缩小了整个网页以适应屏幕。

借助 HTML 中的视口标签，可以控制视口参数，例如高度、宽度、比例和分辨率。

使用元标记对于匹配页面与设备屏幕的兼容性至关重要。 在设计页面时，设计人员根据视口可以处理的内容来布置页面中的元素。

如果没有这个元标记，设备会以实际的、更高的分辨率呈现，然后尝试通过缩放将其放入更小的移动浏览器中。 这会导致设计被破坏并且事物看起来被缩小了。 通过添加此元标记，您可以告诉您要在这个较小的浏览器视口中呈现它，然后可以通过 CSS 中的媒体查询将其作为目标。

这是元标记的示例。

 <meta name=”viewport” content=”width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">

媒体查询

使用它们，您可以根据屏幕宽度调整尺寸。 但要使其在移动设备上运行，您需要使用前面提到的元标记。

您可以尝试使用这些查询分别针对每个设备，但这太困难了，而且遗憾的是没有可用于移动或桌面版本的固定分辨率大小。 您唯一可以做的就是研究，并找出大多数用户使用的设备。

您必须在正确的设备上进行识别和测试。

话虽这么说，您通常希望拥有两个或三个不同的类别并尝试定位它们——台式机、移动设备和平板电脑。 一旦确定了设备目标，就需要确定和定义响应式设计断点。

其他测试工具

一旦您的应用程序设计完成，您就可以实施它。 您确实希望确保一切仍按预期工作。 这需要在不同的浏览器、操作系统和屏幕尺寸上进行大量测试。 一种方法是使用浏览器检查器工具，该工具通常使用 f12 键打开。 然而，它们只配备了一些内置屏幕和互联网速度的版本。 如果你想要一些开箱即用的好工具，你可以考虑 BrowserStack Live。 使用它，您不仅可以测试您的应用程序在不同网速下的行为，还可以在不同的分辨率和操作系统（如 Android v2 和新版 Windows 11）中进行测试。

结论

如果您打开这篇文章希望得到银弹解决方案，很抱歉让您失望了。 哪有这回事。 今天唯一的共识是先为移动设计，然后为桌面调整。 但最重要的是，谁在使用您的应用程序，以及如何使用。 如果您的应用程序用作公司软件，其中 90% 或更多的用户通过公司笔记本电脑访问它，那么首先为桌面构建它是合乎逻辑的。 您仍然需要在他们的屏幕上进行适当的研究。 之后，您可以使用上述方法对其进行适当的结构化和实现。

如需更多信息，您可以在Twitter 、 LinkedIn 、 GitHub或Instagram上关注我。



原文:

When websites and apps were created a bit over 10 years ago, no one even thought of mobile web applications. Good screens were at best if they were full HD, and very rarely people would use something like 24 inches or 27 inches’ screen size.

Today, the situation is much different. There are a whole range of handheld devices and most of them have higher resolution when compared to what was there a few years ago.

Device screens have evolved, resolutions have increased, not only that but you also now have wide screens, curved screens, and diagonal sizes going well over 40 inches. This all comes with a new set of possibilities but also a new set of problems that developers and testers need to solve. The more the device fragmentation increases so does the need to create a responsive web design that will render properly on different resolutions.

In this guide, you will find top responsive web design challenges and their solutions.

Viewport
