Viewport is the visible area for the user . It varies from device to device and is smaller on a mobile phone when compared to a desktop.

Earlier webpages were consumed only from the desktop. Then, people started surfing the internet using tablets and mobile phones. At this point fixed size web pages were too large to fit the viewport. To fix this, browsers on those devices scaled down the entire web page to fit them to the screen.

With the help of the viewport tag in HTML, one can control the viewport parameters such as height, width, scale and resolution.

Using the meta tag is essential to match the page compatibility to the device screen. When designing a page, designers lay out the elements in the page based on what the viewport can handle.

What happens without this meta tag is that the device renders on the actual, much higher resolution, and then tries to fit it into the much smaller one of the mobile browser by scaling. This results in broken design and things appearing zoomed out. By adding this meta tag, you tell that you want to render it in this smaller browser viewport, which can be then targeted by media queries in CSS.

Here is an example of the meta tag.

<meta name=”viewport” content=”width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">

Media queries

Media queries in CSS are used to apply CSS rules to specific targets. With them, you could adjust sizes depending on screen width. But for this to work on mobile devices, you would need to use the previously stated meta tag.

You could try to target every device separately with these queries, but that would be too difficult, and sadly there is no fixed resolution size you could use for mobile or desktop versions. The only thing you could do is research, and figure out which devices most of your users use.

BrowserStack has compiled a list of devices frequently used by users across the globe. It is essential that you identify and test on the right devices.

Now with that being said, you usually want to have two or three different categories and try to target them — Desktop, mobile and tablet. Once you identify your device targets you need to identify and define a responsive design breakpoint.

Other testing tools

Once your application design is done, and you implement it. You do want to ensure everything still works as expected. And that requires a lot of testing on different browsers, operating systems and screen sizes. One way to do it is by using the browser inspector tool, which is usually opened by using the f12 key. However, they only come with a few built in screen and internet speed versions. If you want some good tool that comes with everything out of the box, you might consider BrowserStack Live. Using it, not only you could test your application behavior with different internet speed, but also in different resolutions and operating systems as old as Android v2, and as new as Windows 11.

Conclusion

If you opened this article hoping to have a silver bullet solution, I am sorry to disappoint you. There is no such thing. Today’s only kind of consensus is to design first for mobile, and adjust for desktop. But most important is, who is using your application, and how. If your application is used as a company software where 90% or more of the users access it by company laptop, it is logical to build it first for desktop. You would still need to make proper research on their screens. After that, you can use the above-mentioned methods to properly structure it and implement it.

For more, you can follow me on Twitter, LinkedIn, GitHub, or Instagram.

item.title: 我创建了 100 多个独特的 CSS 模式 | 最好的合集🤩 | I created 100+ unique CSS patterns | The best collection 🤩
item.description:
在CSS Loaders和CSS Underline/Overlay 动画之后，我带着另一个大集合回来了。 这次是关于 CSS 模式。

我从头开始编写了 100 多个独特的 CSS 模式（是的，这花了我很多时间）。

所有模式都具有相同的代码结构：

 html { --s: 50px; /* control the size */ --c1: red; --c2: blue; /* more colors if needed */ /* A lot of gradients and strange numbers*/ }

您有一个变量来控制大小，并且每种颜色都在代码中定义一次。 您只需单击一下即可复制/粘贴代码并调整几个变量。

抓住一个漂亮的图案，并在您最喜欢的网站上向我展示结果👇

我不会在本文中列出所有 CSS 模式，但这是我的前 10 名

最好的

我真的很喜欢这一个。 只用了2个渐变，效果还是很满意的，尤其是那个小阴影。

不可能的事

这个有 15 个渐变！ 我认为这是不可能的，但经过大量的数学计算和头痛之后，我做到了。

意外

我试图创建一个特定的模式，在计算中犯了一个错误，但我以一个更好的模式结束。 一个不错的意外！

六边形

每个人都喜欢六边形，所以我必须在前 10 名中有一个

我做了一些 3D 图案，但这一个真的很酷

视错觉

线条是直的吗？ 不要相信你的大脑！

具有挑战性的

这一个中使用了很多几何技巧。 这是一个很好的挑战。

波浪形

如果你不喜欢六边形，你一定喜欢波浪。

完美的

我在CSS Tip上使用这个，结果很完美。

圣诞节

如果你不知道圣诞礼物

你呢？ 说出你最喜欢的👇

如果您想开始使用一些基本模式，这里有 2 篇适合初学者的文章：

通过构建背景图案来学习 CSS 径向渐变

以后我会有更多的文章。 我将揭示制作复杂 CSS 模式的所有秘密，敬请期待👀

该集合将在未来不断增长，所以不要忘记为网站添加书签： css-pattern.com

所有代码都在Github中，所以点击那个 ⭐ Star 让我们让这个项目流行起来。

你想支持我吗？

或者



原文:

After the CSS Loaders and the CSS Underline/Overlay animations, I am back with another big collection. This time it's about CSS Patterns.

👉 CSS Pattern 👈

More than 100 unique CSS patterns coded from scratch by me (Yes, It took me a lot of time).

All the patterns have the same code structure:

html { --s: 50px; /* control the size */ --c1: red; --c2: blue; /* more colors if needed */ /* A lot of gradients and strange numbers*/ }

You have one variable to control the size and each color is defined once in the code. All you have to do is one click to copy/paste the code and adjust a few variables.

Grab a nice pattern and show me the result on your favorite website 👇

I will not list all the CSS patterns in this article but here are my Top 10

The Best

I really like this one. Only 2 gradients are used and the result is quite satisfying, especially that little shadow.

The Impossible

15 Gradients for this one! I thought it was an impossible one but after a lot of math and headaches, I did it.

The Accident

I was trying to create a specific pattern, made a mistake in the calculation and I end with a better one. A nice accident!

The Hexagon

Everyone like Hexagons so I must have one in the Top 10

The 3D

I made a few 3D patterns but this one is really cool

The Optical Illusion

Are the lines straight? Don't trust your brain!

The Challenging

A lot of geometry tricks are used in this one. It was a good challenge.

The Wavy

If you don't like hexagons, you must like waves.

The Perfect

I am using this one on CSS Tip and the result is perfect.

The Christmas

In case you have no idea of gifts for Christmas

What about you? Tell me your favorite one 👇

Here are 2 beginner-friendly articles I wrote if you want to get started with some basic patterns:

Learn CSS radial-gradient by Building Background Patterns

How to create background pattern using CSS & conic-gradient

I will have more articles in the future. I will reveal all the secrets of making complex CSS Patterns so stay tuned 👀

The collection will keep growing in the future so don't forget to bookmark the site: css-pattern.com

All the code is in Github so go click that ⭐ Star and let's make the project popular.

item.author: afif

item.title: 使用 API 网关链接 API 请求 | Chaining API requests with API Gateway
item.description:
随着需要集成的 API 数量的增加，管理 API 交互的复杂性变得越来越具有挑战性。 通过使用API 网关，我们可以创建一系列 API 调用，将 API 工作流程分解为更小、更易于管理的步骤。 例如，在在线购物网站中，当客户搜索商品时，平台可以向商品搜索 API 发送请求，然后向商品详情 API 发送请求以检索有关商品的更多信息。 在本文中，我们将为Apache APISIX API 网关创建一个自定义插件，以处理应按顺序调用的客户端请求。

学习目标

您将在整篇文章中了解到以下内容：

  • 什么是链接 API 请求？
  • 顺序 API 调用示例。
  • 如何为 Apache APISIX 构建自定义管道请求插件。
  • 管道请求插件演示。

什么是 chaining API 请求，我们为什么需要它？

链接 API 请求（或管道请求，或顺序 API 调用）是软件开发中使用的一种技术，用于管理 API 交互的复杂性，其中软件需要多次 API 调用才能完成一项任务。 它类似于批处理请求处理，您将多个 API 请求组合成一个请求，然后将它们作为批处理发送到服务器。 虽然它们看起来很相似，但管道请求涉及向服务器发送单个请求，触发一系列 API 请求以定义的顺序执行。 序列中的每个 API 请求都可以修改请求和响应数据，一个 API 请求的响应将作为输入传递给序列中的下一个 API 请求。 当客户端需要执行一系列必须按特定顺序执行的相关 API 请求时，管道请求会很有用。

在管道的每个步骤中，我们都可以在将响应数据传递到下一步之前对其进行转换或操作。 这在需要将数据返回给客户端之前对其进行规范化或转换或过滤掉敏感数据的情况下很有用。 它可以帮助减少整体延迟。 例如，一个 API 调用可以在另一个 API 等待响应时调用，从而减少完成工作流所需的总时间。

我们将使用Apache APISIX，因为它是一种流行的开源 API 网关解决方案，带有一堆内置插件。 但是，在撰写当前博文时，APISIX 还没有对管道请求插件的官方支持。 通过了解 APISIX 的自定义插件开发功能，我们决定引入一个可以提供相同功能的新插件。 使用此插件，您可以指定应按顺序调用的上游 API 列表，以处理单个客户端请求。 每个上游 API 都可以修改请求和响应数据，并且来自一个上游 API 的响应作为输入传递给管道中的下一个上游 API。 管道可以在路由配置中定义，您还可以在管道执行 API URL 时定义它们的顺序。

让我们通过一个例子来了解这个插件的用法。 假设您有两个 API - 一个发出 GET /credit_cards请求以检索信用卡信息，另一个在 POST /filter请求正文中接收先前的响应数据，然后过滤掉敏感数据（例如信用卡号和过期时间） date) 在将响应返回给客户端之前。 因为信用卡 API 端点返回不应暴露给未授权方的敏感信息。 下图说明了整体数据流：

  1. 当客户端向 API 网关的信用卡 API 端点请求检索所有信用卡信息时，API 网关转发请求以从信用卡后端服务检索信用卡数据。
  2. 如果请求成功并返回信用卡数据，则它传递给管道中的下一步安全服务
  3. 当从安全服务接收到过滤后的响应时，它将组合的响应返回给客户端。

管道请求插件演示

对于此演示，我们将利用 GitHub 上另一个准备好的演示项目，您可以在其中找到本教程中使用的所有curl 命令示例，运行 APISIX 并启用自定义插件，而无需使用Docker compose .yml 文件进行额外配置。

先决条件

  • 您还可以使用Postman等简单工具与 API 进行交互。

第 1 步：安装并运行 APISIX 和 etcd

您可以通过在 fork/clone项目后从项目根文件夹运行docker compose up来轻松安装 APISIX 和 etcd。 您可能会注意到在docker-compose.yml文件中指定了一个卷./custom-plugins:/opt/apisix/plugins:ro 。 这将安装本地目录* ./custom-plugins *，其中我们的pipeline-request.lua文件与自定义插件实现作为路径/opt/apisix/plugins docker 容器中的只读卷。 这允许在运行时将自定义插件添加到 APISIX（此设置仅适用于使用 docker 运行 APISIX 的情况）。

第 2 步：使用 pipeline-request 插件创建第一个 Route

配置的重要部分是“插件”部分，它指定“管道请求”插件应该用于此 API 路由。 插件配置包含一个“节点”数组，它定义了应该在管道中执行的 API 请求的顺序。 您可以在那里定义一个或多个 API。 在这种情况下，管道由两个节点组成：第一个节点向random-data-api.com/api/v2/credit_cards ****API 发送请求以检索信用卡数据，第二个节点发送请求到127.0.0.1:9080/filter的本地 API，以从信用卡信息中过滤掉敏感数据。 第二个 API 将只是一个使用无服务器预函数APISIX 插件的无服务器函数。 它充当后端服务来修改来自第一个 API 的响应。

第 3 步：使用无服务器插件创建第二个路由

接下来，我们配置一个 ID 为 2 的新路由，用于处理对管道中/filter端点的请求。 它还启用无服务器预函数APISIX 的现有插件，我们在其中指定应由插件执行的 Lua 函数。 此函数只是从先前的响应中检索请求正文，替换信用卡号字段，并保持响应的其余部分不变。 最后，它将当前响应主体设置为修改后的请求主体，并将 HTTP 200 响应发送回客户端。 您可以修改此脚本以满足您的需要，例如使用解码后的主体执行进一步的处理或验证。

 curl -X PUT 'http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/routes/2' \ --header 'X-API-KEY: edd1c9f034335f136f87ad84b625c8f1' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data-raw ' { "uri": "/filter", "plugins":{ "serverless-pre-function": { "phase": "access", "functions": [ "return function(conf, ctx) local core = require(\"apisix.core\") local cjson = require(\"cjson.safe\") -- Get the request body local body = core.request.get_body() -- Decode the JSON body local decoded_body = cjson.decode(body) -- Hide the credit card number decoded_body.credit_card_number = \"****-****-****-****\" core.response.exit(200, decoded_body); end" ] } } }'

第 3 步：测试设置

现在是测试整体配置的时候了。 使用下面的 curl 命令，我们向端点http://127.0.0.1:9080/my-credit-cards发送一个 HTTP GET 请求。

 curl http://127.0.0.1:9080/my-credit-cards

我们在第二步中配置了相应的路由，以使用具有两个节点的pipeline-request插件，此请求将触发管道从https://random-data-api.com/api/v2/credit_cards检索信用卡信息https://random-data-api.com/api/v2/credit_cards端点，使用http://127.0.0.1:9080/filter端点过滤敏感数据，并将修改后的响应返回给客户端。 查看输出：

 { "uid":"a66239cd-960b-4e14-8d3c-a8940cedd907", "credit_card_expiry_date":"2025-05-10", "credit_card_type":"visa", "credit_card_number":"****-****-****-****", "id":2248 }

如您所见，它将请求正文中的信用卡号（实际上，它是链中第一个 API 调用的响应）替换为星号。

概括

到目前为止，我们了解到我们的 Apache APISIX API 网关自定义管道请求插件允许我们将一系列 API 调用定义为特定顺序的管道。 我们还可以将这个新插件与现有插件结合使用，为我们的 API 端点启用身份验证、安全性和其他 API 网关功能。

相关资源

推荐内容

社区

关于作者

访问我的博客： www.iambobur.com



原文:

As the number of APIs that need to be integrated increases, managing the complexity of API interactions becomes increasingly challenging. By using the API Gateway we can create a sequence of API calls, which breaks down the API workflows into smaller, more manageable steps. For example, in an online shopping website when a customer searches for a product, the platform can send a request to the product search API, then send a request to the product details API to retrieve more information about the products. In this article, we will create a custom plugin for Apache APISIX API Gateway to handle client requests that should be called in sequence.

Learning objectives

You will learn the following throughout the article:

  • What are chaining API requests?
  • Example of sequential API calls.
  • How to build a custom pipeline-request plugin for Apache APISIX.
  • Pipeline-request plugin demo.

What is a chaininng API request and why do we need it?

Chaining API requests (or pipeline requests, or sequential API calls) is a technique used in software development to manage the complexity of API interactions where software requires multiple API calls to complete a task. It is similar to batch request processing where you group multiple API requests into a single request and send them to the server as a batch. While they may seem similar, a pipeline request involves sending a single request to the server that triggers a sequence of API requests to be executed in a defined order. Each API request in the sequence can modify the request and response data, and the response from one API request is passed as input to the next API request in the sequence. Pipeline requests can be useful when a client needs to execute a sequence of dependent API requests that must be executed in a specific order.

In each step of the pipeline, we can transform or manipulate the response data before passing it on to the next step. This can be useful in situations where data needs to be normalized or transformed or filter out sensitive data before it is returned to the client. It can help to reduce overall latency. For example, one API call can be made while another is waiting for a response, reducing the overall time needed to complete the workflow.

Custom pipeline-request plugin for Apache APISIX

An API gateway can be the right place to implement this functionality because it can intercept all client app requests and forward them to intended destinations. We are going to use Apache APISIX as it is a popular open-source API Gateway solution with a bunch of built-in plugins. However, at the time of developing the current blog post, APISIX did not have official support for the pipeline-request plugin. By having the knowledge of custom plugin development capabilities of APISIX, we decided to introduce a new plugin that can offer the same feature. There is a repo on GitHub with source code written in Lua programming language and a description of the pipeline-request plugin.

With this plugin, you can specify a list of upstream APIs that should be called in sequence to handle a single client request. Each upstream API can modify the request and response data, and the response from one upstream API is passed as the input to the next upstream API in the pipeline. The pipeline can be defined in a Route configuration, and you can also define orders for API URLs when the pipeline should execute them.

Let’s understand the usage of this plugin with an example. Suppose you have two APIs - one that makes GET /credit_cards requests to retrieve credit card information and another that receives previous response data in the body of POST /filter request and then it filters out the sensitive data (such as the credit card number and expiration date) before returning the response to the client. Because the credit card API endpoint returns sensitive information that should not be exposed to unauthorized parties. The below diagram illustrates the overall data flow:

  1. When a client makes a request to the credit card API endpoint of API Gateway to retrieve all credit card info, API Gateway forwards a request to retrieve the credit card data from the credit card backend service.
  2. If the request is successful and returns credit card data, then it passes to the next step in the pipeline which security service
  3. When the filtered response is received from the security service, it returns the combined response to the client.

Pipeline-request plugin demo

For this demo, we are going to leverage another prepared demo project on GitHub where you can find all curl command examples used in this tutorial, run APISIX and enable a custom plugin without additional configuration with a Docker compose.yml file.

Prerequisites

  • Docker is used to installing the containerized etcd and APISIX.
  • curl is used to send requests to APISIX Admin API. You can also use easy tools such as Postman to interact with the API.

Step 1: Install and run APISIX and etcd

You can easily install APISIX and etcd by running docker compose up from the project root folder after you fork/clone the project. You may notice that there is a volume ./custom-plugins:/opt/apisix/plugins:ro specified in docker-compose.yml file. This mounts the local directory*./custom-plugins* where our pipeline-request.lua file with the custom plugin implementation as a read-only volume in the docker container at the path /opt/apisix/plugins. This allows custom plugins to be added to APISIX in the runtime (This setup is only applicable if you run APISIX with docker).

Step 2: Create the first Route with the pipeline-request plugin

Once APISIX is running, we use cURL command that is used to send an HTTP PUT request to the APISIX Admin API /routes endpoint to create our first route that listens for URI path /my-credit-cards.

curl -X PUT 'http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/routes/1' \
--header 'X-API-KEY: edd1c9f034335f136f87ad84b625c8f1' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{ "uri":"/my-credit-cards", "plugins":{ "pipeline-request":{ "nodes":[ { "url":"https://random-data-api.com/api/v2/credit_cards" }, { "url":"http://127.0.0.1:9080/filter" } ] } }
}'

The important part of the configuration is the "plugins" section, which specifies that the "pipeline-request" plugin should be used for this API route. The plugin configuration contains a "nodes" array, which defines the sequence of API requests that should be executed in the pipeline. You can define one or multiple APIs there. In this case, the pipeline consists of two nodes: the first node sends a request to the random-data-api.com/api/v2/credit_cards ****API to retrieve credit card data, and the second node sends a request to a local API at 127.0.0.1:9080/filter to filter out sensitive data from the credit card information. The second API will be just a serverless function using the serverless-pre-function APISIX plugin. It acts just as a backend service to modify the response from the first API.

Step 3: Create the second Route with the serverless plugin

Next, we configure a new route with the ID 2 that handles requests to /filter endpoint in the pipeline. It also enables serverless-pre-function APISIX’s existing plugin where we specify a Lua function that should be executed by the plugin. This function simply retrieves the request body from the previous response, replaces the credit card number field, and leaves the rest of the response unchanged. Finally, it sets the current response body to the modified request body and sends an HTTP 200 response back to the client. You can modify this script to suit your needs, such as by using the decoded body to perform further processing or validation.

curl -X PUT 'http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/routes/2' \
--header 'X-API-KEY: edd1c9f034335f136f87ad84b625c8f1' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '
{ "uri": "/filter", "plugins":{ "serverless-pre-function": { "phase": "access", "functions": [ "return function(conf, ctx) local core = require(\"apisix.core\") local cjson = require(\"cjson.safe\") -- Get the request body local body = core.request.get_body() -- Decode the JSON body local decoded_body = cjson.decode(body) -- Hide the credit card number decoded_body.credit_card_number = \"****-****-****-****\" core.response.exit(200, decoded_body); end" ] } }
}'

Step 3: Test setup

Now it is time to test the overall config. With the below curl command, we send an HTTP GET request to the endpoint http://127.0.0.1:9080/my-credit-cards.

curl http://127.0.0.1:9080/my-credit-cards

We have the corresponding route configured in the second step to use the pipeline-request plugin with two nodes, this request will trigger the pipeline to retrieve credit card information from the https://random-data-api.com/api/v2/credit_cards endpoint, filter out sensitive data using the http://127.0.0.1:9080/filter endpoint, and return the modified response to the client. See the output:

{ "uid":"a66239cd-960b-4e14-8d3c-a8940cedd907", "credit_card_expiry_date":"2025-05-10", "credit_card_type":"visa", "credit_card_number":"****-****-****-****", "id":2248
}

As you can see, it replaces the credit card number in the request body (Actually, it is the response from the first API call in the chain) with asterisks.

Summary

Up to now, we learned that our custom pipeline request plugin for the Apache APISIX API Gateway allows us to define a sequence of API calls as a pipeline in a specific order. We can also use this new plugin with the combination of existing ones to enable authentication, security, and other API Gateway features for our API endpoints.

Related resources

Recommended content

Community

🙋 Join the Apache APISIX Community
🐦 Follow us on Twitter
📝 Find us on Slack
💁 How to contribute page

About the author

Visit my blog: www.iambobur.com
item.author: bobur

item.title: 使用这 5 个 JavaScript 最佳实践优化您的代码🚀🔥 | Optimise your code with these 5 JavaScript best practices 🚀🔥
item.description:
首先，我们有变量、函数和其他代码结构的命名约定。 该指南不仅涉及外观或含义，而且还极大地影响了代码的可读性和有效的调试。

在 JavaScript 中，建议对变量和函数（例如，myVariableName）使用驼峰式大小写，对类（例如，MyClassName）使用 Pascal 大小写。

 // ❌ Poorly named variables: let a = 'John'; let fn = () => console.log('Hello'); // ✅ Descriptive variable names: let firstName = 'John'; let sayHello = () => console.log('Hello');

虽然速记技术提供了一种更快、更整洁的代码编写方式，但务必要谨慎，因为它们可能会产生意想不到的结果。 为避免此类不可预见的结果，必须查阅文档、探索相关的 JavaScript 代码示例并彻底测试输出。

 // ❌ Traditional function declaration: function square1 (num) { return num * num } // ✅ Using arrow functions (shorthand): const square2 = num => num * num // ❌ Very long code: let x if (y) { x = y } else { x = 'default' } // ✅ A more succinct way to achieve the same result using logical OR: let x = y || 'default'

为了简单和组织，建议避免使用 JavaScript 直接应用样式。 这个被称为关注点分离 (SoC) 的原则建议使用 classList API 添加或删除类，同时使用 CSS 定义样式规则。

通过遵循这种方法，CSS 负责样式任务，而 JavaScript 则专注于处理应用程序中的其他功能。

值得注意的是，关注点分离 (SoC) 的概念超出了 JavaScript 的范围，并且作为一种实践来分离功能并防止不同技术的混合。

总之，CSS 应该处理与 CSS 相关的任务，而 JavaScript 应该避免处理样式问题。

 // ❌ Avoid using JavaScript for styling: let element = document.getElementById('my-element') element.style.color = 'red' // ✅ Changing styles by adding/removing classes: let element = document.getElementById('my-element') element.classList.add('my-class')

与函数相反，JavaScript 中的类不进行提升，这意味着您必须在调用类之前声明它。 最初，这可能看起来违反直觉，特别是如果您熟悉函数提升。 然而，这是在 JavaScript 中使用类时应该理解和遵循的基本原则。

 // ❌ Calling a class before declaration: const hat = new Hat('Red', 1000) hat.show() class Hat { constructor (color, price) { this.color = color this.price = price } show () { console.log(`This ${this.color} hat costs $${this.price}`) } } // ✅ Calling a class after declaration: class Hat { constructor (color, price) { this.color = color this.price = price } show () { console.log(`This ${this.color} hat costs $${this.price}`) } } const hat = new Hat('Red', 1000)

初学者犯的最普遍的错误之一是过多的块嵌套，他们将一个块放在另一个块中，例如 for 循环在另一个循环中，在 try-catch 块中的 if-else 语句中，等等。

结果，代码变得杂乱无章，难以理解其功能或找到负责特定任务的特定代码。 调试此类代码可能会让人不知所措且困难重重，而且还可能使遇到它的其他程序员感到困惑。 此外，这种做法会给人一种不专业的印象。

为了缓解这些问题，避免过度嵌套并争取更清晰、更有条理的代码结构至关重要。

 // ❌ Nesting code blocks too much and not using the return keyword function checkNumber (num) { if (num > 0) { console.log('Number is positive.') } else { if (num < 0) { console.log('Number is negative.') } else { console.log('Number is zero.') } } } // ✅ Using the return keyword instead of the else statement function checkNumber (num) { if (num > 0) { console.log('Number is positive.') return } if (num < 0) { console.log('Number is negative.') return } console.log('Number is zero.') }

我一直对写作充满热情，没有什么比为他人提供帮助和启发更让我开心的了。 如果您有任何疑问或需要任何指导，请随时与我联系。 我是来帮忙的！

我的 Instagram - @arjuncoder
我的 Github - @CoderPOOP
我的推特 - @arjuncodes



原文:

First, we have the naming convention for variables, functions, and other code structures. This guideline is not only about appearance or meaning, but it also greatly impacts code readability and effective debugging.

In JavaScript, it is recommended to use camel-case for variables and functions (e.g., myVariableName) and Pascal case for classes (e.g., MyClassName).

// ❌ Poorly named variables:
let a = 'John';
let fn = () => console.log('Hello'); // ✅ Descriptive variable names:
let firstName = 'John';
let sayHello = () => console.log('Hello');

2. Use Shorthands but Be Cautious

While shorthand techniques offer a quicker and neater way to write code, it's important to be cautious as they can yield unexpected results. To avoid such unforeseen outcomes, it is essential to consult the documentation, explore relevant JavaScript code examples, and thoroughly test the output.

// ❌ Traditional function declaration:
function square1 (num) { return num * num
}
// ✅ Using arrow functions (shorthand):
const square2 = num => num * num // ❌ Very long code:
let x if (y) { x = y
} else { x = 'default'
} // ✅ A more succinct way to achieve the same result using logical OR:
let x = y || 'default'

3. Follow the SoC principle

For simplicity and organization, it is advisable to avoid using JavaScript for directly applying styling. This principle, known as separation of concerns (SoC), suggests utilizing the classList API to add or remove classes, while defining style rules with CSS.

By following this approach, CSS takes care of styling tasks, while JavaScript focuses on handling other functionalities within your application.

It's important to note that the concept of separation of concerns (SoC) extends beyond JavaScript and serves as a practice to separate functionalities and prevent the mixing of different technologies.

In summary, CSS should handle CSS-related tasks, while JavaScript should refrain from dealing with styling concerns.

// ❌ Avoid using JavaScript for styling:
let element = document.getElementById('my-element')
element.style.color = 'red' // ✅ Changing styles by adding/removing classes:
let element = document.getElementById('my-element')
element.classList.add('my-class')

4. Understand the Lack of Hoisting in Classes

In contrast to functions, classes in JavaScript do not undergo hoisting, which implies that you must declare a class before invoking it. Initially, this might appear counterintuitive, especially if you're familiar with function hoisting. However, it is an essential principle that should be comprehended and followed when working with classes in JavaScript.

// ❌ Calling a class before declaration:
const hat = new Hat('Red', 1000)
hat.show()
class Hat { constructor (color, price) { this.color = color this.price = price } show () { console.log(`This ${this.color} hat costs $${this.price}`) }
} // ✅ Calling a class after declaration:
class Hat { constructor (color, price) { this.color = color this.price = price } show () { console.log(`This ${this.color} hat costs $${this.price}`) }
} const hat = new Hat('Red', 1000)

5. Avoid the Over-Nesting in your code

One of the most prevalent errors made by beginners is excessive nesting of blocks, where they place a block within another block, such as a for loop inside another loop inside an if-else statement within a try-catch block, and so on.

As a result, the code becomes cluttered, making it challenging to understand its functionality or locate the specific code responsible for a particular task. Debugging such code can be overwhelming and difficult, and it may also confuse other programmers who come across it. Additionally, this practice can convey an unprofessional impression.

To mitigate these issues, it is crucial to avoid excessive nesting and strive for cleaner, more organized code structure.

// ❌ Nesting code blocks too much and not using the return keyword
function checkNumber (num) { if (num > 0) { console.log('Number is positive.') } else { if (num < 0) { console.log('Number is negative.') } else { console.log('Number is zero.') } }
} // ✅ Using the return keyword instead of the else statement
function checkNumber (num) { if (num > 0) { console.log('Number is positive.') return } if (num < 0) { console.log('Number is negative.') return } console.log('Number is zero.')
}

I have always been deeply passionate about writing, and nothing brings me more joy than offering assistance and inspiration to others. If you have any inquiries or require any guidance, please don't hesitate to contact me. I am here to help!

My Instagram - @arjuncoder
My Github - @CoderPOOP
My Twitter - @arjuncodess
item.author: arjuncodess

item.title: 作为新手浏览新的代码基础 | Navigating a new Code Basis as a Newbie
item.description:
我的一个新学生做得很好。 他们当前的任务是了解代码基础并为此实现一些较小的功能。 我喜欢他们的地方 - 每当有不清楚的地方时，他们都会问我。 到目前为止，他们提出了非常好的问题！ 然而，他们中的大多数是他们自己可以找到答案的问题，他们只需再投入 10 分钟，这就是如何......

急躁的诅咒❤️‍🔥

学生充满动力和活力，因此希望在完成任务时感受到这种奖励感。 正因为如此，他们很快就会变得不耐烦。 这对我来说并不新鲜，事实上，当我刚开始作为开发人员工作时，我就像他们一样。

我记得我的导师是如何逐渐厌倦我问一些我可以很容易地自己回答的问题，这不是因为他们不是一个好导师，而是他们也有公平的工作要做。 那时我并没有真正看到有什么大不了的，现在我站在他的立场上，这没什么大不了的。 问题是上下文切换我的导师，现在我必须经历这从你身上吸取了很多能量。

打破习惯⛏️

因此，对于急于明天部署下一个功能的人来说，多花 10 分钟听起来是合理且易于管理的。 然而，这 10 分钟应该如何度过呢？ 正如我之前告诉过你的，我和他们一样，现在这些是我在出门询问同事之前要做的事情：

零。 退后一步：从办公桌前站起来，看看窗外一分钟，喝杯咖啡——做点什么来打破你心中的紧张感。

当我必须实现一个需要在多个类中进行更改的更复杂的功能时，我会在每个类中添加注释，概述在这里和那里必须完成的工作。 然后，在开始实际实施之前，我开始创建一堆自动化测试。

此过程帮助我大致了解必须实现的内容以及代码的去向。 我看到的一个很大的好处是我可以估计我脑海中的想法在理论上是否可行。 因此，为我的实际实施提供了良好的开端。

到目前为止，在我工作的团队中，习惯在每个提交消息的开头添加票号。 有了这些有价值的信息，人们就可以打开他们的票务系统并搜索这张特定的票。 如果您知道当时的要求是什么，那么您可能会理解为什么代码是这样的。

如果有的话，它们通常会很好地概述代码打算用于哪些真实案例。查看代码的使用方式以及调用应该使用的数据应该会给你一个很好的理解领域逻辑的抽象。

提示：在某些情况下，当我事先查看与此代码相关的工单时，我什至在认为合适时自己添加了一些测试。 这样我就可以检查我是否正确理解了需求，我们甚至可以对代码进行更多测试，这总是一件好事。

当我这样做时，我会在日常会议中提及它并为其创建一个单独的合并请求。 在我的团队中，很高兴看到这样的工作，但在这样做之前请先与您的同事核实一下。

通过它们，您可以查看在哪个时间点执行了哪些片段以及抛出了哪些参数。 从这个意义上说，您在日常生活中使用的任何类型的调试也可以用于发现。

如果我似乎没有解开围绕我需要使用的代码的谜团，我只是开始删除其中的一些代码。 通过这种方式，您可以看到哪些测试现在可能会失败，哪些行为会有所不同，哪些事情会停止存在。

我希望这些技巧可以帮助一个或另一个开发人员、学生或导师。 就我而言，按顺序完成所有这些操作，直到找到我一直在寻找的东西大约需要 10 到 20 分钟，但经过多年的例行公事，这并不奇怪。 所以，如果您需要比这更长的时间，请不要担心！

在这方面你有什么建议？ 我很想听听你的意见并改进我的日常工作！

注意：当在我们心爱的dev.to上进一步研究这个主题时，我还发现了Nicolas Frankel写的很好的帖子。 如果你想要更多关于这个主题的提示，也可以去看看那个帖子！



原文:

A new pupil of mine does a great job. Their current task is to get to know the code basis and implement some smaller features into it in order to do so. What I like about them - They asked me whenever something is unclear. And they asked very good questions so far! However, most of them are questions for which they could find the answers themselves would they only invest 10 more minutes, and here is how ...

The Curse of Impatience ❤️‍🔥

The pupil is full of drive and energy, and thus wants to feel this sense of reward whenever one completes a task. Because of this, they grow impatience pretty fast. And this is nothing new to me, in fact I was just like them when I first started working as a developer.

I remember how my mentor grew progressively tired of me asking questions that I could have easily answered by myself, not because they weren't a good mentor, yet they also had their fair share of work to do. Back then I didn't really see the big deal and now that I am in his shoes, there isn't a big deal. The problem is the context switching my mentor and now I have to go through that sucks a lot of energy from you.

Breaking the Habit ⛏️

So, investing 10 more minutes sounds reasonable and managable for someone eager to deploy their next feature tomorrow. Yet how should these 10 minutes be spend? As I told you before I have been just like them and nowadays are these the things I do in order before going forth and asking a collogue about it:

Zero. Take a Step Back: Get up from your desk, look out of the window for a minute, get a coffee - do something to break that tense feeling in your mind.

1. Planning Before Coding: This is something I always do by now. When I have to implement a more complex feature requiring changes in multiple classes a add comments in each of them outlining what has to be done here and there. Then I start creating a bunch of automated tests before a start the acutal implementation.

This procedure helps me to get an overview of what I have to implement and where the code has to go. A big benefit I see with this is that I can estimate wheter what I had in my mind would theoretically work or not. Therefore, giving me a good head start for the actual implemetation.

2. Git Blame: And no, I don't want someone to screenshot some piece of code, sending it to someone with a message reading: "What did you think when writing this?". In the teams I worked in so far it was a custom to add the ticket number at the beginning of each commit message. With this valuable info one can just open up their ticketing system and search for this specific ticket. If you know what the requirements back then where you will probaly understand why the code is the way it is.

3. Look at the Tests Implemeted: Another way to understand what a piece of code was meant to be doing is to take a look at the implemented tests. Given there are any, they usually give a good overview of what real-life cases the code was intented to be used in. Looking at how the code is used and with which data the calls are supposed to be working should give you a good graps of the domain logic abstracted.

Tip: In some intances, as I looked at the ticket related to this code beforehand, I even add some tests myself when I see them as fitting. This way I can check wheter I understood the requirements correctly and we even get more tests for the code which is always a good thing.

When I do it, I mention it in our daily meeting and create a separte merge request for it. In my team such work is gladly seen, yet check in with your colluges before doing so.

4. Use Break Points: Using the tools provided to us by your IDEs of choice is another option you should consider when stuck in a new code basis. With them you can see which snippets are executed at which point in time and what parameters are thrown all about. In this sense, any kind of debugging you are using in your daily life might also be used in a discovering sense.

5. Break it: Moving a little further away from this conservative approaches - I would claim that we all work with some kind of source control. If I don't seem to unravel the mysteries surrounding the code I need to work with, I simply start removing some of it. That way you can see what test may fail now, which things behave differently and which things simply stopped existing.

I hope these tips may help one or the other developer, pupil or mentor. On my part doing all of them in order until I found what I have been looking for takes about 10 to 20 minutes, yet having years and years of routine with it, this is not suprising. So, don't worry if it takes you a little longer than that!

What are your tips in this regard? I would love to hear them from you and improve my one routine!

Cover image by Yan Krukau! 🥳

Note: When looking into this topic a bit more on our beloved dev.to I also found this well-written post by Nicolas Frankel. Go checkout that post as well if you want even more tips on this topic!
item.author: jessica_veit

item.title: 了解为什么我们呼吁在 PostgreSQL 中使用索引 | Learn Why we call to use index with PostgreSQL
item.description:
为了更好地理解成本估算过程在 PostgreSQL 中的工作原理，探索它如何影响查询执行的实际示例会很有帮助。 在之前的文章中，我们讨论了计划器、执行器、顺序扫描和运行时扫描在查询执行中的作用。 要将这些概念应用到实际场景中，建议查看之前关于 PostgreSQL 中的顺序扫描和成本估算的文章。

什么是索引

与普通表不同，索引存储在为快速插入和搜索而优化的专用数据结构中，例如 B-tree、GiST 或 Hash。

这允许基于索引列中的值快速搜索和检索数据。

通过允许 PostgreSQL 比扫描整个表更有效地定位和检索特定行，索引可以显着提高查询性能。 然而，重要的是要注意索引并不总是适用于所有情况的最佳解决方案。 在某些情况下，维护索引的开销可能超过性能优势。 因此，仔细考虑要包含在索引中的列并监控查询的性能以确保索引提供预期的性能改进非常重要。

让我们写一些SQL

我创建了一个 PostgreSQL 函数，它通过获取用户输入的行数和表名来生成行。 使用此功能，我能够在几秒钟内生成超过 1000 万行。 接下来，我执行了几个查询来观察它们的性能。

 CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION generate_series(n integer,name text ,t text) RETURNS void AS $BODY$ DECLARE i integer; BEGIN FOR i IN 1..n LOOP EXECUTE 'INSERT INTO ' || quote_ident(t) || ' VALUES (' || i || ',' || quote_literal(name || i) || ')'; END LOOP; END; $BODY$ LANGUAGE plpgsql VOLATILE COST 100;
 EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT id from t where name='NAME'; CREATE INDEX ON t(id); RESET ALL; EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT id from t where t.id = 1000; EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT name from t where t.id = 2000;

第一个查询花费的时间最长，因为它使用顺序扫描根据文本列过滤行。 第二个和第三个查询在主键列上使用了仅索引扫描，从而提高了查询性能。

结果

第一个将最长的查询作为

Gather (cost=1000.00..11614.43 rows=1 width=10) (actual time=1.348..46.516 rows=1 loops=1) Workers Planned: 2 Workers Launched: 2 -> Parallel Seq Scan on t (cost=0.00..10614.33 rows=1 width=10) (actual time=24.932..39.442 rows=0 loops=3) Filter: (name = 'NAME10000'::text) Rows Removed by Filter: 333333 Planning Time: 0.058 ms Execution Time: 46.533 ms

在 PostgreSQL 中分析查询性能时，EXPLAIN ANALYZE 命令提供了对查询执行过程的宝贵见解。 此命令的输出包括规划器使用的工作线程数、执行的扫描类型（例如顺序或仅索引）、查询的规划时间和查询的执行时间等信息。

虽然 PostgreSQL 可以使用并行线程从数据库中读取数据并提高性能，但不应将其作为优化不佳查询的解决方案。 重要的是编写干净、优化的查询以避免给 CPU 带来过度的负载。

查询的计划时间可能会根据其复杂性和优化情况而有所不同，但优化查询的计划时间应该不会显着影响总执行时间。 执行时间是从表中检索数据所需的时间，是查询性能的关键因素。

建议将重点放在优化查询以改善其执行时间，而不是仅仅依赖并行性或增加硬件资源。 通过优化查询并仔细选择要包含在索引中的列，可以在 PostgreSQL 中实现显着的性能改进。

 Index Only Scan using t_id_idx on t (cost=0.42..8.44 rows=1 width=4) (actual time=0.832..0.834 rows=1 loops=1) Index Cond: (id = 100000) Heap Fetches: 1 Planning Time: 0.060 ms Execution Time: 0.873 ms

当真正不需要索引时

有两种主要情况，第一种是针对表本身，另一种是针对查询

1- table 1- 表



原文:

To better understand how the cost estimation process works in PostgreSQL, it can be helpful to explore practical examples of how it impacts query execution. In previous articles, we discussed the role of the planner, executor, sequential scan, and runtime scan in query execution. To apply these concepts to real-world scenarios, it's recommended to review the previous article on sequential scans and cost estimation in PostgreSQL.

What is an index

An index in PostgreSQL is similar to an index in a book or a set of notes, where it provides a way to quickly locate and retrieve specific information. Unlike normal tables, indexes are stored in a specialized data structure optimized for quick insertion and searching, such as B-tree, GiST, or Hash.

The most common type of index in PostgreSQL is the balanced-tree index, or B-tree, which efficiently stores and organizes data in a tree-like structure. This allows for fast searching and retrieval of data based on the values in the indexed column(s).

Indexes can significantly improve query performance by allowing PostgreSQL to locate and retrieve specific rows more efficiently than scanning the entire table. However, it's important to note that indexes are not always the optimal solution for all cases. In some cases, the overhead of maintaining the index can outweigh the performance benefits. Therefore, it's important to carefully consider the columns to include in an index and monitor the performance of your queries to ensure that the indexes are providing the expected performance improvements.

Let's write some SQL

I have created a PostgreSQL function that generates rows by taking user input for the number of rows and table name. With this function, I was able to generate over 10 million rows within seconds. Next, I executed several queries to observe their performance.

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION generate_series(n integer,name text ,t text)
RETURNS void AS
$BODY$
DECLARE i integer;
BEGIN FOR i IN 1..n LOOP EXECUTE 'INSERT INTO ' || quote_ident(t) || ' VALUES (' || i || ',' || quote_literal(name || i) || ')'; END LOOP;
END;
$BODY$
LANGUAGE plpgsql VOLATILE
COST 100;

EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT id from t where name='NAME';
CREATE INDEX ON t(id);
RESET ALL;
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT id from t where t.id = 1000; EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT name from t where t.id = 2000;

The first query took the longest time as it used a sequential scan to filter the rows based on a text column. The second and third queries used an index-only scan on the primary key column, resulting in much faster query performance.

Results

the first one will take the longest query as

Gather (cost=1000.00..11614.43 rows=1 width=10) (actual time=1.348..46.516 rows=1 loops=1) Workers Planned: 2 Workers Launched: 2
 -> Parallel Seq Scan on t (cost=0.00..10614.33 rows=1 width=10) (actual time=24.932..39.442 rows=0 loops=3)
 Filter: (name = 'NAME10000'::text) Rows Removed by Filter: 333333 Planning Time: 0.058 ms Execution Time: 46.533 ms

When analyzing query performance in PostgreSQL, the EXPLAIN ANALYZE command provides valuable insights into the query execution process. The output of this command includes information such as the number of worker threads used by the planner, the type of scan performed (such as sequential or index-only), the planning time for the query, and the execution time for the query.

While PostgreSQL can use parallel threads to read data from the database and improve performance, this should not be relied on as a solution for poorly optimized queries. It's important to write clean, optimized queries to avoid putting undue load on the CPU.

The planning time for a query can vary depending on its complexity and optimization, but an optimized query should have a planning time that does not significantly impact the total execution time. The execution time is the time needed to retrieve the data from the table and is a critical factor in query performance.

It's recommended to focus on optimizing queries to improve their execution time, rather than relying solely on parallelism or increasing hardware resources. By optimizing queries and carefully selecting which columns to include in indexes, it's possible to achieve significant performance improvements in PostgreSQL.

 Index Only Scan using t_id_idx on t  (cost=0.42..8.44 rows=1 width=4) (actual time=0.832..0.834 rows=1 loops=1)
   Index Cond: (id = 100000)
   Heap Fetches: 1
 Planning Time: 0.060 ms
 Execution Time: 0.873 ms

when index is not really needed

there are two main situations the first is realted to the table itself and the other is realted to the query

1- table
item.author: nightbird07

item.title: 在终端中发出很酷的命令 | Make your cool commands in terminal
item.description:
阿尔轩艾莉亚

发表于 5 月 25 日

嗨，大家好，
我想教你如何在你的终端中制作很酷的东西，并像专业人士一样提高你的工作效率。

对于本文，您不需要任何 docker、k8s、Openshift 或 Golang 经验，我们只是将它们用作示例，但在其他情况下您也会明白这一点。

长话短说

打开您的 shell 配置文件（例如 Mac 中的~/.zshrc ）
像这样写别名命令alias mycmd="cmd long long long"

和功能是这样的：

 mf() { mkdir $1 cd $1 touch $2 }

并在终端中这样调用它： mf mydir myfile它将创建一个名为mydir的目录和其中名为myfile的文件

这篇文章背后的故事

作为一名开发人员，您可能会经常使用 CLI，CLI 被设计为通用和可组合的，但在某些特定情况下，您可能希望永远按照您的意愿组合它们！

当我在 Snapp 工作时，我需要一些特定的oc命令组合供日常使用。

此外，我需要使用 2 个不同客户端的 2 个不同 VPN 连接，它们的命令很长！

有时它们很长，有时它们很难记住，等等。

定义别名

这是一个替代命令，您可以使用它来缩短命令。

就我而言，我想像这样使用 openfortivpn 客户端连接到 VPN：

 sudo openfortivpn vpn.host.domain:port -u myusername -p mypassword --trusted-cert averyveryveryverylonghash

另一个使用 openconnect 客户端的 VPN：

 sudo openconnect -u myusername -p mypassword vpn.host.domain:port

除此之外，我工作的每个公司或个人项目，我都使用docker-compose up --build -d命令，它有什么作用？ 没关系！

对于本文，我们只知道这些命令在大多数情况下都是相同的，并且输入它们很麻烦。

长话短说，像这样缩短这些命令：

 alias mycmd="my very very long command"

但它不是永久性的，它仅限于当前的 shell 选项卡，要让它永远存在于您的系统中，请打开您的 shell 配置并在其中写入您的别名。

因此，根据这个，在我的系统（MacOS）中，我遵循了以下步骤：

  • 第 1 步：运行code ~/.zshrc （ code打开 VScode 编辑器，但您可以使用其他工具，例如 vim 或 nano）
  • 第 2 步：在打开的文件中写入此命令并保存：
 alias dub="docker-compose up --build -d`
  • 第 3 步：大功告成！ 打开一个新的 shell并使用dub命令，它的工作原理完全相同。

关于 VPN：

 alias ofv="sudo openfortivpn vpn.host.domain:port -u myusername -p mypassword --trusted-cert averyveryveryverylonghash" alias opc="sudo openconnect -u myusername -p mypassword vpn.host.domain:port"

我定义的另一个别名：

 alias ocp="oc port-forward"

定义函数

有时，您需要定义多个命令，或者您需要在命令中包含一些占位符以保持这些部分仍然是通用的。

比方说，有时您需要一些函数来执行不同的操作并接受一些输入

一些例子

当我想实例化一个新的 Golang 项目时，我应该运行这些命令：

 mkdir <my_project_name> cd <my_project_name> touch main.go go mod init <my_project_name>

其余的可能不同，但这些命令总是相同的。

对于本文的这一部分，我也有更好的解决方案，但它们不在本文的上下文中，这只是一个示例。

因此，为了简化命令并使它们成为一个命令，请编写如下函数：

 gomake() { mkdir $1 cd $1 touch main.go go mod init $1 }

我将此函数称为gomake ，我可以使用gomake <my_project_name>在我的 shell 中轻松访问它，它将执行这 4 个命令。

另一个例子，使用oce <pod-name>而不是oc exec <pod-name> -it -- sh

 oce() { oc exec $1 -it -- sh }

另一个带有 2 个参数的示例，有时我想查看有关项目的结构和一些很酷的信息（例如代码行数）

对于这种情况，我知道两个命令：

我总是忘记第二个！ 因此，为了帮助自己记住第二个命令和第一个命令标志，我编写了这个函数并将其命名为struct

 struct() { tree $1 -dCL $2 -I vendor cocnt search $1 }

关于树标志， I用于排除文件夹（在我的例子中是供应商）， d用于仅显示目录， L用于进入内部的级别（我们作为参数获得）， C用于为输出着色。

所以我调用struct <project-path> 2 ，它会打印出没有供应商文件夹的二级目录的项目。

只是如果你想知道（用过的术语）

要了解有关 bash 函数的更多信息：
结论

除了 fish 或 warp 等所有现代终端工具之外，这些别名和函数仍然有用。

除此之外，有时我会编写自己的 CLI 工具或使用现有工具，

在制作项目（本文中的 Golang）的情况下，模板非常有用且是很好的选择。

可能性是无限的...

最后，希望您喜欢阅读本文并学到一些有用的东西。

一旦暂停，arshamalh 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，arshamalh 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，arshamalh 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 arshamalh 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 arshamalh：

降低 arshamalh 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 arshamalh 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Arsham Arya

Posted on May 25

   

Hi guys,
I wanna teach you how to make cool things in your terminal and increase your productivity like a pro.

You don't need any docker, k8s, Openshift or Golang experience for this article, we just used them as examples but you'll get the point in other cases too.

TL;DR

Open your shell config file (e.g. ~/.zshrc in Mac)
Write aliases commands like this alias mycmd="cmd long long long"

and functions like this:

mf() { mkdir $1 cd $1 touch $2
}

And call it in terminal like this: mf mydir myfile and it will make a directory named mydir and file within it named myfile

The story behind this article

As a developer, you may work with CLIs a lot, CLIs are made to be general and composable, but in some specific cases, you may want to compose them as you wish once forever!

At the time I was working at Snapp, I needed some specific compositions of oc command for everyday use.

Also, I needed 2 different VPN connections using 2 different clients, they had long commands!

Sometimes they are long, sometimes they are hard to remember, etc.

Define aliases

There is an alias command available on *nix systems (Linux, Unix, MacOS, etc)

It's an alternative command which you can use to make your commands shorter.

In my case, I wanted to connect to a VPN with openfortivpn client like this:

sudo openfortivpn vpn.host.domain:port -u myusername -p mypassword --trusted-cert averyveryveryverylonghash

And another VPN using openconnect client:

sudo openconnect -u myusername -p mypassword vpn.host.domain:port

In addition to that, every company or personal project that I worked for, I used docker-compose up --build -d command, what does it do? doesn't matter!

For this article, we just know that these commands are the same most of the time and typing them out is cumbersome.

Long story short, shorten these commands like this:

alias mycmd="my very very long command"

But it's not permanent, it's limited to the current shell tab, to make it live forever in your system, open your shell config and write your aliases in it.

So according to this, in my system (MacOS), I followed these steps:

  • Step 1: run code ~/.zshrc (code opens up VScode editor, but you can use something else such as vim or nano)
  • Step 2: write this command inside the opened file and save it: 
alias dub="docker-compose up --build -d`
  • Step 3: you are done! open up a new shell and use dub command and it works totally the same.

And about VPNs:

alias ofv="sudo openfortivpn vpn.host.domain:port -u myusername -p mypassword --trusted-cert averyveryveryverylonghash"

alias opc="sudo openconnect -u myusername -p mypassword vpn.host.domain:port"

Another alias I defined:

alias ocp="oc port-forward"

Define functions

Sometimes, you need to define more than one command or you need to have some placeholders in your commands to keep those parts still general purpose.

Let's say, sometimes you need some functions to do different operations and accept some inputs

Some examples

When I want to instantiate a new Golang project, I should run these commands:

mkdir <my_project_name> 
cd <my_project_name>
touch main.go
go mod init <my_project_name>

The rest might be different but these for commands are always the same.

I also have better solutions for this part of the article, but they are not in the context of this article and it's just an example.

So, to simply that for commands and make them one, write a function like below:

gomake() {
    mkdir $1 
    cd $1
    touch main.go
    go mod init $1
}

I called this function gomake and I can easily access it in my shell using gomake <my_project_name> and it will do that 4 commands.

$1 is a function argument, if you need more, you can access them by $2, $3, ...

Another example, using oce <pod-name> instead of oc exec <pod-name> -it -- sh

oce() {
    oc exec $1 -it -- sh
}

Another example with 2 arguments, Sometimes I want to see the structure and some cool information about the projects (e.g. number of lines of code)

For this case, I know two commands:

tree command, which is a tool to show directory structure of your projects, and it's pre-installed or you may install it using brew install tree in mac.

cocnt which is a tool to count the number of lines of code, link to their github repo: github.com/InnoFang/code-counter

And I always forget the second! 😂 (what was it's command?)

So for helping myself remembering the second command and first command flags, I wrote this functions and named it struct

struct() {
    tree $1 -dCL $2 -I vendor
    cocnt search $1
}

About tree flags, I is used for excluding a folder (vendor in my case), d to show only directories, L for level of going inside (which we get as an argument), C for colorizing the output.

So I call struct <project-path> 2 and it will print out the project with level two of directories without vendor folder.

Just if you are wondering (Used terms)

oc is command used for working with Openshift clusters.

Openshift is a platform made on top of Kubernetes for container orchestrations.

Docker is a platform designed to help developers build, share, and run modern applications.

To learn more about bash functions:
devqa.io/create-call-bash-functions/

Conclusion

These aliases and functions are still useful beside all modern terminal tools such as fish or warp, etc.

In addition to that, sometimes I write my own CLI tools or I use existing ones,

In case of making a project (Golang in this article), templates are so useful and good alternative.

Possibilities are endless...

Finally, I hope you enjoyed reading this article and learned something useful.

Eamonn Cottrell - Apr 30 '22

Once suspended, arshamalh will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, arshamalh will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by arshamalh will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If arshamalh is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag arshamalh:

Make all posts by arshamalh less visible

arshamalh consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging arshamalh will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: arshamalh

item.title: 通过有限责任公司最大限度地节税：探索优点、缺点和策略 | Maximizing Tax Savings with an LLC: Exploring the Pros, Cons, and Strategies
item.description:
简介：
对于小企业所有权，成立有限责任公司 (LLC) 可以提供显着的税收优惠。 了解节税潜力以及相关的利弊对于寻求优化财务状况的企业家来说至关重要。 在本文中，我们将深入探讨有限责任公司节税的利弊，并探索最大化您的税收优势的可能策略。

有限责任公司的节税优势：

  1. 转嫁税：
    有限责任公司的转嫁税收功能是一个主要优势。 它允许企业的利润和损失直接流向个人成员，他们在个人纳税申报表上报告。 通过避免传统公司发生的双重征税，有限责任公司确保收入仅在个人层面征税。 这可以节省大量税款，因为个人税率通常低于公司税率。

  2. 灵活的利润分配：
    有限责任公司为成员分配利润提供了相当大的灵活性。 这意味着可以战略性地分配收入，利用成员之间不同的税级。 通过将利润分配给较低税级的成员，可以最大限度地减少 LLC 及其成员的整体纳税义务。 必须确保利润分配合理且符合税法，以避免任何潜在的审查。

  3. 扣除和业务费用：
    有限责任公司可以扣除普通和必要的业务费用，这可以大大减少应税收入。 可扣除的费用可能包括租金、水电费、办公用品、员工工资、营销费用等。 正确记录和维护这些费用的准确记录对于支持审计时的扣除至关重要。

  4. 自雇税收节省：
    有限责任公司最重要的优势之一是可以节省自雇税。 与公司雇员为其工资缴纳社会保障税和医疗保险税不同，有限责任公司成员仅需就其利润份额缴纳自雇税。 这使得剩余的收入可以免除这些额外的税款，从而产生潜在的储蓄。

  5. 资本收益处理：
    当 LLC 出售增值资产（如房地产或投资）时，成员可能有资格享受资本收益待遇。 与普通所得税率相比，这可能导致较低的税率。 具体的资本利得率取决于资产的持有期限和会员的整体收入水平等因素。 利用资本利得处理可能是减少纳税义务的宝贵策略。

有限责任公司节税的缺点：

  1. 自雇税：
    虽然有限责任公司可以提供自雇税收优惠，但重要的是要考虑自雇税收的影响。 会员有责任就其利润份额缴纳社会保障税和医疗保险税。 这些税收可以抵消从转嫁税收结构中获得的一些税收优惠。

  2. 州税费：
    有限责任公司需缴纳各州特定的税费，具体税费因司法管辖区而异。 一些州对有限责任公司征收附加税、年费或特许经营税，从而减少了整体节税。 研究和了解您运营所在州的纳税义务至关重要。

  3. 有限损失扣除：
    美国国税局的规则限制成员没有实质性参与的被动活动损失的扣除。 这意味着，如果您投资有限责任公司但不积极参与其运营，您从其他收入中扣除损失的能力可能有限。 积极参与是充分利用损失扣除收益的关键。

可能的前进方向：

为了有效应对 LLC 节税的复杂性，寻求合格税务专业人士的指导至关重要。 专门从事小企业税务的税务顾问或会计师可以根据您的独特情况提供个性化建议。 他们会考虑诸如您的收入水平、

一旦暂停，balor44 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，balor44 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，balor44 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 balor44 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 balor44：

降低 balor44 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 balor44 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

   

Introduction:
When it comes to small business ownership, forming a Limited Liability Company (LLC) can offer significant tax advantages. Understanding the tax savings potential, along with the associated pros and cons, is crucial for entrepreneurs seeking to optimize their financial position. In this article, we will delve into the benefits and drawbacks of tax savings from an LLC and explore possible strategies for maximizing your tax advantage.

Pros of Tax Savings from an LLC:

  1. Pass-through Taxation:
    An LLC's pass-through taxation feature is a major advantage. It allows the profits and losses of the business to flow directly to the individual members, who report them on their personal tax returns. By avoiding double taxation, which occurs with traditional corporations, an LLC ensures that income is taxed only at the individual level. This can lead to significant tax savings as individual tax rates are often lower than corporate tax rates.

  2. Flexible Profit Allocation:
    LLCs offer members considerable flexibility in allocating profits. This means that income can be distributed strategically, taking advantage of varying tax brackets among members. By allocating profits to members in lower tax brackets, the overall tax liability of the LLC and its members can be minimized. It is essential to ensure that profit allocations are reasonable and compliant with tax laws to avoid any potential scrutiny.

  3. Deductions and Business Expenses:
    LLCs can deduct ordinary and necessary business expenses, which can substantially reduce taxable income. Deductible expenses may include rent, utilities, office supplies, employee salaries, marketing costs, and more. Properly documenting and maintaining accurate records of these expenses is crucial to support deductions in the event of an audit.

  4. Self-employment Tax Savings:
    One of the most significant advantages of an LLC is the potential for self-employment tax savings. Unlike employees of a corporation who pay Social Security and Medicare taxes on their salaries, LLC members are subject to self-employment taxes only on their share of the profits. This allows the remaining income to be exempt from these additional taxes, resulting in potential savings.

  5. Capital Gains Treatment:
    When an LLC sells appreciated assets, such as real estate or investments, members may qualify for capital gains treatment. This can lead to lower tax rates compared to ordinary income tax rates. The specific capital gains rates depend on factors such as the holding period of the asset and the member's overall income level. Utilizing capital gains treatment can be a valuable strategy for minimizing tax obligations.

Cons of Tax Savings from an LLC:

  1. Self-employment Taxes:
    While LLCs can provide self-employment tax savings, it is important to consider the self-employment tax implications. Members are responsible for paying Social Security and Medicare taxes on their share of the profits. These taxes can offset some of the tax benefits derived from the pass-through taxation structure.

  2. State Taxes and Fees:
    LLCs are subject to state-specific taxes and fees, which vary depending on the jurisdiction. Some states impose additional taxes, annual fees, or franchise taxes on LLCs, reducing the overall tax savings. It is essential to research and understand the tax obligations in your state of operation.

  3. Limited Loss Deduction:
    The IRS has rules that restrict the deductibility of losses from passive activities in which members do not materially participate. This means that if you invest in an LLC but do not actively engage in its operations, you may have limited ability to deduct losses against other income. Active involvement is key to fully leverage the loss deduction benefits.

Possible Way Forward:

To navigate the complexities of tax savings from an LLC effectively, seeking guidance from a qualified tax professional is crucial. A tax advisor or accountant specializing in small business taxation can provide personalized advice based on your unique circumstances. They will consider factors such as your income level, the nature of

VyacheslavKonyshev - May 25

item.title: 按您的方式编写代码：自信地自定义 Amplication 的代码 | Code Your Way: Customizing Amplication's Code With Confidence
item.description:
出于多种原因，全世界的开发人员都依赖 Amplication。 首先，Amplication 在构建支持其应用程序的所有服务时为他们节省了时间和精力。 我们通过处理所有样板和配置为他们节省时间，同时为他们提供基于行业信任的库和技术构建的可靠、人类可读的代码。

开发人员可以使用他们喜爱和信任的开源技术构建后端，例如 Node.js、NestJS、Prisma 等！ 更好的是，开发人员可以根据他们的需要完全自定义他们的后端，因为我们生成实际代码，而不仅仅是一个黑盒子。

刚接触 Amplication 的开发人员确实会提出一个问题，“当我需要进行更改（例如添加数据模型和角色或更新权限）时，它如何处理我的定制服务？” 这是一个合理的担忧。 你的代码会被覆盖吗？ 你能告诉 Amplication 不要触及特定文件吗？ 我们如何处理将更新后的代码合并到您的自定义代码中？

好吧，这些问题对我来说很容易回答。 首先，请放心，我们会避免覆盖您的代码。 其次，Amplication 是完全可定制的，如果您希望我们保留您的文件，我们会这样做。 最后，我们巧妙地合并事物并始终尝试保持事物同步，这样您的代码就永远不会落后。

在本文中，我们将深入探讨 Amplication 如何可靠地更新您的服务，以便您永远不必担心您编写的代码被覆盖。 包括了：

  • 使用可定制文件
  • 使用.amplicationignore
  • 智能 Git 策略

最后，为了扩展定制代码的整个概念，我们将分享一些关于 Amplication 插件生态系统的信息，这样您就可以比以前更多地定制您的服务！

使用可定制文件

我们为您在 Amplication 中定义的每个实体生成一个唯一的模块。 该模块有一些与之关联的文件：

  • 用于处理 REST API 调用的控制器。
  • 用于处理 GraphQL API 调用的解析器。
  • 一种服务，它是 Prisma 客户端的抽象，用于调用您的数据库。

上图显示了为实体“Customer”生成的模块和相关文件。

这些文件供开发人员添加他们的自定义逻辑，因为它们是 Amplication 生成的控制器、解析器和服务的抽象。 如果您查看这些文件中的任何一个，您会发现它们都是从其自身的基础版本扩展而来的。 因此，例如，查看CustomerService ，您可以看到它是从CustomerServiceBase扩展而来的。

单击进入CustomerServiceBase可以看到 Amplication 为您生成的与数据库交互的逻辑。

这些可自定义的文件是一种单向应用，使开发人员可以放心地进行编码，他们不会失去所有的辛勤工作。 如果您想更深入地了解如何使用这些可自定义的文件，请转至有关向您的服务添加自定义代码的Amplication 文档文章。

使用 .ampliationignore

在使用 Amplication 时，您可能有希望我们避免接触的文件。 例如，假设在 Amplication 生成代码后，您想要锁定实体服务的基础。 问题是文件的所有基本版本都是 Amplication 的域，也是我们通常使用的域。

在这种情况下，您可以使用.amplicationignore文件。 使用与.gitignore文件类似的语法，开发人员可以防止任何文件被 Amplication 修改，无论这些文件位于何处。 与.gitignore一样，您应该将.amplicationignore文件放在存储库的根目录中。

这是.amplicationignore的示例：

 server/src/customer/base/CreateCustomerArgs.ts

下次 Amplication 根据您的需要更新您的项目时， CreateCustomerArgs.ts文件将保持不变。 但是，如果您想实现 Amplication 所做的更改，那没关系； 我们会将生成的文件放入.amplication文件夹中，这不会混淆您的代码。

您可以在我们的文档中了解有关.amplicationignore文件的更多信息。

智能 Git 策略

此功能适用于具有 Pro 计划的工作区，而具有免费计划的工作区可以访问不太高级的版本。

通过 SGS，Amplication 创建一个amplication分支，用于通过拉取请求服务创建新的提交。 因此，一次只会打开一个拉取请求； 这样做可以确保我们避免冲突。

这种方法的主要好处是用户不再需要解决每个构建的合并冲突，从而更容易使用 Amplication。

插件

在前面的部分中，我们深入探讨了如何扩展单个应用程序。 但是，如果您需要对许多项目进行相同的更改怎么办？ 插件系统是解决方案。 插件可以为现有服务或新服务添加新功能，只需单击一下即可安装或启用。

您可以使用我们的社区 Amplication Team 开发的插件，或构建您自己的插件！

如果插件听起来令人兴奋，您可以在此处找到我们可用插件的列表。 如果您准备好开发自己的插件，请查看我们文档中的插件开发概述。

结论

今天，我们回顾了多种将自定义逻辑添加到 Amplication 生成的应用程序的方法。 我希望您有信心通过 Amplication 开始您的下一个技术挑战。 如果您有更多问题或需要更多信息，请加入我们的Discord 频道，访问我们的文档，并阅读更多我们优秀的博客文章。

编码愉快！



原文:

Developers worldwide depend on Amplication for many reasons. First, Amplication saves them time and effort when building all the services that power their applications. We save them time by handling all the boilerplate and configurations while giving them reliable, human-readable code built on top of industry-trusted libraries and technologies.

Developers get backends built with open-source technologies they love and trust, like Node.js, NestJS, Prisma, and more! Better yet, developers can fully customize their backend based on their needs because we generate actual code, not just a black box.

One question that does spring up for devs new to Amplication is, "How does it handle my customized services when I need to make changes like adding data models and roles or updating permissions?" It's a valid concern. Does your code get overwritten? Can you tell Amplication not to touch specific files? How do we handle merging our updated code into your customized code?

Well, these are easy questions for me to answer. First, rest assured that we avoid overwriting your code. Second, Amplication is fully customizable, and if you want us to keep your files, we will. Finally, we merge things smartly and always try to keep things in sync so your code never falls behind.

In this post, we'll dive into how Amplication reliably updates your services so that you never need to worry about the code you write being overwritten. That includes:

  • Using Customizable Files
  • Using .amplicationignore
  • Smart Git Strategy

And finally, to expand on the whole idea of customized code, we'll share a bit about the Amplication plugin ecosystem so you can customize your services even more than before!

Using Customizable Files

We generate a unique module for every Entity that you define in Amplication. The module has a few files associated with them:

  • A controller for handling REST API calls.
  • A resolver for handling GraphQL API calls.
  • A service that's an abstraction for the Prisma client used to make calls to your database.

The image above shows a module and associated files generated for the entity "Customer.”

These files exist for developers to add their custom logic because they're an abstraction from the controller, resolver, and service that Amplication generates. If you look at any of those files, you'll see that they all extend from a base version of itself. So, for example, looking at the CustomerService, you can see that it extends from the CustomerServiceBase.

Clicking into the CustomerServiceBase lets you see the logic that Amplication generates for you to interact with the database.

These customizable files are one-way Amplication allows developers to code with confidence that they won't lose all their hard work. If you want a deeper dive into using these customizable files, head over to the Amplication docs article about adding custom code to your services.

Using .ampliationignore

While working with Amplication, you may have files you want us to avoid touching. For example, imagine that after Amplication generates code, you want to lock the base of an entity's service. The problem is all base versions of files are Amplication's domain and what we usually work in.

In this case, you can use the .amplicationignore file. Using a similar syntax to a .gitignore file, developers can prevent any file, no matter where these files are located, from being modified by Amplication. Like .gitignore, you should place your .amplicationignore file in the root of a repository.

Here is an example of .amplicationignore:

server/src/customer/base/CreateCustomerArgs.ts

The next time Amplication updates your project based on whatever you need, the CreateCustomerArgs.ts file will be untouched. However, if you wanted to implement the changes Amplication would have made, that's ok; we'll put the file we would have generated into a .amplication folder, which won't mess with your code.

You can learn more about the .amplicationignore file in our docs.

Smart Git Strategy

Amplication's Smart Git Strategy (SGS) is designed to prevent conflicts between the code users write in their projects and the code generated by Amplication. This functionality is available for workspaces with a Pro plan, while workspaces with a Free plan have access to a less advanced version.

With the SGS, Amplication creates an amplication branch for creating new commits by the pull request service. As a result, only one pull request will open at a time; doing so ensures we avoid conflicts.

The main benefit of this approach is that users no longer have to resolve merge conflicts with every build, making it much easier to work with Amplication.

Plugins

In the previous sections, we dove into how to extend a single app. But what if you need to make the same changes for many projects? The plugin system is the solution. Plugins may add new functionality to existing or new services and can be installed or enabled with just a click.

You can use plugins developed by Amplication Team, our community, or build your own plugins!

If plugins sound exciting, you can find a list of our available plugins here. And if you're ready to develop your own plugins, check out the Plugin Development Overview in our docs.

Conclusion

Today we've reviewed multiple ways to add your custom logic to apps that Amplication generated. I hope you feel confident starting your next tech challenge with Amplication. If you have more questions or need more information - join our Discord channel, visit our docs, and read more of our excellent blog articles.

Happy coding!
item.author: michaelsolati

item.title: 什么是 Jai 编程语言？ | What is Jai Programming Language?
item.description:
在本文中，我们将探讨 Jai 编程语言的全部内容、它的特性，以及它为何变得越来越流行。

它旨在成为一种高性能和专注于生产力的语言，用于游戏编程和其他性能密集型应用程序。 让我们仔细看看 Jai 的一些主要功能：

内存管理和垃圾收集

静态打字

静态类型允许 Jai 在编译时提供有关变量、函数和程序中其他类型表达式的类型信息。 这允许及早发现类型错误，这不仅使代码更清晰，而且更易于调试。

现代语法和语言特性

它还具有接口、契约、泛型和其他编程范例。

内置工具支持

该语言通过其内置的工具支持支持高效的调试、分析和优化。

为什么 Jai 编程语言变得流行？

以下是 Jai 编程语言在游戏行业变得流行的一些原因：

表现

该语言使游戏系统编程变得简单、高效，并且比 C++ 更省时。 它的异步垃圾收集器使得无需实现性能或内存管理就可以可靠地编写高性能程序成为可能。 此功能使 Jai 成为寻求优化性能和效率的游戏开发工作室的有吸引力的选择。

干净的语法

它的语法简洁易读，允许程序员编写更清晰、结构更好的代码。

模具支持

该语言旨在简化开发过程，并附带高效的调试器、分析器和优化工具，使开发人员能够高效地调试和优化他们的代码。

简化的开发过程

它提供现代语法、异步垃圾收集器及其本地工具支持。 这些功能使 Jai 成为需要高水平生产力的游戏开发工作室的有吸引力的选择。

结论

它旨在解决游戏开发中遇到的问题，重点是性能和生产力。 该语言提供现代语法、工具支持和改进的安全功能。 随着技术的发展，毫无疑问，Jai 有机会在编程世界中脱颖而出，成为一种与其他编程语言竞争激烈的语言。



原文:

Jai is a programming language that has been gaining popularity across the tech industry, particularly within the gaming industry. Created by game developer Jonathan Blow, Jai is a high-performance programming language that aims to simplify coding and improve productivity. In this article, we will explore what Jai programming language is all about, its features, and why it is becoming increasingly popular.

Jai is a general-purpose programming language that was created by American game developer Jonathan Blow. It is designed to be a high-performance and productivity-focused language for game programming and other performance-intensive applications. Jai is an open-source programming language that is still in the development phase, but it has already caught the attention of many programmers and game developers.

Jai programming language is designed to be faster, safer, and more productive than C++. Blow believes that many of the difficulties encountered when programming in C++ could have been avoided if the language design had prioritized programmer productivity and safety. Jai's syntax is inspired by C and C++, but with many modern features added to make programming easier.

Features of Jai Programming Language

Jai is designed to offer several features that make it more productive and easier to use than other similar languages. Let's take a closer look at some of the key features of Jai:

Memory Management and Garbage Collection

Jai programming language adopts automatic memory management to keep the language free from manual memory management, which can be a cause of crashes and hard to detect bugs. Automatic memory management in Jai is asynchronous, making it possible for the program to be written without the burden of a garbage collector cycle.

Static Typing

Static typing allows Jai to provide type information about variables, functions, and other types of expressions in a program during compile-time. This allows for early detection of type errors, which not only makes the code cleaner but also easier to debug.

Modern Syntax and Language Features

Jai programming language has many modern syntax features such as function-like properties, type inference, and cleaner syntax for attributes. It also features interface, contract, generics, and other programming paradigms.

Inbuilt Tooling Support

Jai is designed to offer robust inbuilt tooling support to enhance productivity. The language supports efficient debugging, profiling, and optimization with its inbuilt tooling support.

Why is Jai Programming Language Becoming Popular?

Jai is rapidly becoming popular because of its features and the problems it aims to solve. Here are some reasons why Jai programming language is becoming popular in the gaming industry:

Performance

Jai is designed to be a fast and high-performance programming language. The language makes programming game systems easy, efficient, and less time-consuming than C++. Its asynchronous garbage collector makes it possible to write high-performance programs reliably without having to implement performance or memory management. This feature makes Jai an attractive choice for game development studios looking to optimize performance and efficiency.

Clean Syntax

Jai offers modern syntax and language features that make it easier to write clean and readable code. Its syntax is concise and readable, allowing programmers to write cleaner and better-structured code.

Tooling Support

Jai comes with robust inbuilt tooling support that enhances productivity. The language aims to simplify the development process and comes with an efficient debugger, profiler, and optimization tools, enabling developers to debug and optimize their code efficiently.

Simplified Development Process

Jai's features make it an effective language for developers interested in simplifying the development process. It offers modern syntax, asynchronous garbage collector, and its native tooling support. These features make Jai an attractive choice for game development studios that require high levels of productivity.

Conclusion

Jai is an open-source programming language that was created by the game developer, Jonathan Blow. It is designed to solve the problems encountered in game development, with a focus on performance and productivity. The language offers modern syntax, tooling support, and improved safety features. As technologies evolve, there is no doubt that Jai has a chance to stand out in the programming world as a language that offers strong competition to other programming languages.

item.title: 要求应该有多详细？🤔 | How detailed should requirements be?🤔
item.description:
我经常收到这样的问题：在与工程师沟通需求时，我应该详细到什么程度，通常要详细到什么程度。 让我总结一下。

这是为那些沟通需求的人而写的。

目标是获得预期的可交付成果

与工程师沟通需求的目的是确保他们了解需要做什么，并因此产生预期的可交付成果。

因此，如果由于信息未包含在需求中而没有产生预期的可交付成果，则可以认为这是沟通需求的人的失败。

从这一点开始思考很重要。
怪自己，不怪别人。

你可能会想，“好吧，我没有在需求中写那个，但是说​​……不是很正常吗？”
但是，如果您接受没有产生预期的可交付成果是因为您没有写一些“正常”的东西，那么继续前进会更容易。

在日常交际中，和亲戚聊天时，你可能会说，“前几天我在东京遇到了叔叔”，或者在工作中和同事聊天时，你可能会说，“我老婆的舅舅住在东京，而且我前几天见过他”。

在日常交流中，我们会改变说话的方式，考虑对方会如何理解。

也就是说，“东京大叔”会被一些人理解，
但有些人不会收到消息，除非你说，“我妻子的母亲的兄弟住在东京”。

重要的是要记住，适当的表达方式会因与您交流的人而异。

投掷易接球

如果有人理解“东京的叔叔”，就不必说“我妻子的母亲的兄弟住在东京”。

另一方面，如果你对“东京叔叔”听不懂的人说“东京叔叔”，他们当然不会收到消息。

向工程师传达需求时也是如此。
您需要确定什么样的表达方式适合工程师理解，并选择合适的表达方式。

结论

  • 沟通需求的目的是让它们产生预期的结果。
  • 如果没有适当地传达需求，就不会产生预期的可交付成果。
  • 为正确传达要求，应使用适当的表达方式。
  • 但是，适当的表达因收件人而异。

我认为在沟通需求时，前三件事很重要。
我想特别强调的是，适当的表达方式因收件人而异。

有许多方法和工具可以有效地表达和交流需求，以及帮助交流需求的相关和间接方法，例如结对编程和代码审查。 其中许多也非常有效。

例如，当你第一次与某人一起工作时，
如果您无法确定什么是合适的，策略可能是采用迭代开发风格来降低试错成本，然后探索传达需求的合适方式。

在组建团队时，您可能还会考虑沟通需求的难易程度。

我将在另一篇文章中写到这些。

一旦暂停，akazah 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，akazah 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

如果 akazah 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

原文:

I often get questions about how detailed I should be when communicating requirements to engineers, and how detailed it's common to be. Let me summarize a bit.

This is written for those who communicate requirements.

The objective is to get the expected deliverables

The purpose of communicating requirements to engineers is to ensure that they understand what needs to be done and that the expected deliverables are produced as a result.

Therefore, if the expected deliverable is not produced because the information was not included in the requirements, it is appropriate to consider this a failure on the part of the person who communicated the requirements.

It is important to start thinking from this point.
Blame yourself, not others.

You may be tempted to think, "Well, I didn't write that in the requirement, but isn't it normal to say..."
(I am tempted to think that way in those situations, too 🫠)

However, it is easier to move on if you accept that the expected deliverables were not produced because you did not write something "normal".

"Uncle in Tokyo"

In everyday communication, when you talk to your relatives, you might say, "I met the uncle in Tokyo the other day" or when you talk to your colleagues at work, you might say, "My wife's mother's brother lives in Tokyo, and I met him the other day".

In everyday communication, we change the way we say things, thinking about how it will be understood by the other person.

In other words, "uncle in Tokyo" will be understood by some people,
But there are others who would not get the message unless you say, "My wife's mother's brother lives in Tokyo".

It is important to remember that the appropriate expression will vary depending on the person you are communicating with.

Throwing Easy-to-Take balls

If someone understands "uncle in Tokyo," there is no need to say, "My wife's mother's brother lives in Tokyo".

On the other hand, if you say, " Uncle in Tokyo" to someone who cannot be understood by "Uncle in Tokyo", of course they will not get the message.

It is the same when communicating requirements to engineers.
You need to determine what kind of expression is appropriate for the engineer to understand, and choose the appropriate expression.

Conclusion

  • The purpose of communicating requirements is to have them produce the expected results.
  • If the requirements are not communicated appropriately, the expected deliverables will not be produced.
  • To properly communicate requirements, appropriate expressions should be used.
  • However, the appropriate expression varies depending on the recipient.

I believe that the first three things are important when communicating requirements.
In particular, I would like to emphasize that the appropriate expression varies depending on the recipient.

There are many ways and tools to efficiently express and communicate requirements, as well as related and indirect means of helping to communicate requirements, such as pair programming and code reviews. Many of them are also highly effective.

For example, when you are working with someone for the first time,
If you are unable to determine what is appropriate, a strategy might be to adopt an iterative development style to lower the cost of trial and error and then explore appropriate ways of communicating requirements.

You might also consider the ease of communicating requirements when building a team.

I will write about those in another article.

(Original Japanese ver.: note.com/akazah/n/n5706310d8ba0)
